abilities.name: Wild Form
abilities.effect: Each time this **MANIFESTATION** is targeted by the ‘Banish Manifestation’ ability, if it would be banished, it is not banished. Instead, allocate 6 damage points to it (ward rolls cannot be made for those damage points).

abilities.name: Devourer of Magics
abilities.effect: If this **MANIFESTATION** destroyed any enemy **MANIFESTATIONS** this turn, **Heal (6)** this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Arcane Predator
abilities.effect: You can add 1 to the number of dice rolled when making charge rolls for this **MANIFESTATION**, to a maximum of 3", while it is within 18" of any enemy **MANIFESTATIONS**, but if you do so, it must end the charge move within ½" of an enemy **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Tendrils of Light and Shadow
abilities.effect: Enemy units cannot use commands while they are within 3" of either part of this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Multiple Parts
abilities.effect:
When a number of damage points equal to this **MANIFESTATION**’s Health characteristic are allocated to it, this **MANIFESTATION** is destroyed and both its parts are removed from play.

Each time this **MANIFESTATION** moves, both its parts must end within 9" of each other.

Each part of this **MANIFESTATION** is armed with **Tendrils of Light and Shadow**.

abilities.name: Arcane Passage
abilities.declare: Pick a friendly **WIZARD** within 3" of this **MANIFESTATION** to be the target.
abilities.effect: The next time the target uses a non-**SUMMON** **SPELL** ability this phase, add 1 to the casting value of that spell. When picking targets for that spell, you can measure range and visibility from either part of this **MANIFESTATION** instead of from the caster, and your opponent can measure range and visibility to either part of this **MANIFESTATION** instead of to the caster for the purposes of the ‘Unbind’ ability.

abilities.name: Multiple Parts
abilities.effect: When a number of damage points equal to this **MANIFESTATION**’s Health characteristic are allocated to it, this **MANIFESTATION** is destroyed and both its parts are removed from play.

abilities.name: Deathly Passage
abilities.declare: Pick a friendly unit wholly within 6" of one part of this **MANIFESTATION** to be the target.
abilities.effect: Remove the target from the battlefield and set it up again on the battlefield wholly within 6" of the other part of this **MANIFESTATION** and more than 9" from all enemy units.

abilities.name: Multiple Parts
abilities.effect: When a number of damage points equal to this **MANIFESTATION**’s Health characteristic are allocated to it, this **MANIFESTATION** is destroyed and both its parts are removed from play.

abilities.name: Mechanisms of Time
abilities.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn, Your Hero Phase
abilities.effect:
If there are any friendly **WIZARDS** within 3" of this **MANIFESTATION**, pick 1 of the following effects:

***Increase Time Flow***: Until the start of your next turn, you can re roll charge rolls for friendly units while they are wholly within 12" of this **MANIFESTATION**.

***Decrease Time Flow***: Until the start of your next turn, subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target friendly **WIZARDS** while they are wholly within 12" of this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Dancing Blades
abilities.effect: Ward rolls cannot be made for damage points inflicted by this **MANIFESTATION**’s attacks.

abilities.name: Scything Blade
abilities.effect: This **MANIFESTATION** can move a distance up to its Move characteristic in one direction (see ‘The Pendulum Swings’). It can pass through models during that move and can end that move in combat. Then, pick up to 3 enemy units that this **MANIFESTATION** passed across during that move or that are within 1⁄2" of it to be the targets. Roll a dice for each target. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.

abilities.name: The Pendulum Swings
abilities.effect: This **MANIFESTATION** cannot use **CHARGE** or **FIGHT** abilities. In addition, when this **MANIFESTATION** moves, it must move in a straight line either in the direction in which the tip of the pendulum blade is pointing or in the opposite direction to the direction in which the tip of the pendulum blade is pointing.

abilities.name: Harbinger of Horror
abilities.declare: Pick an enemy unit that was targeted by this **MANIFESTATION**’s shooting attacks this phase to be the target.
abilities.effect: Roll a dice. If the roll is less than the number of models in the target unit that were slain this phase, the target cannot use commands for the rest of the turn.

abilities.name: Burning Up
abilities.effect: Each time this **MANIFESTATION** uses a **SHOOT** ability, after that ability has been resolved, allocate 1 damage point to this **MANIFESTATION** (ward rolls cannot be made for that damage point).

abilities.name: Morbid Detonation
abilities.declare: You must, and can only, use this ability if this unit has 6 **maelstrom points**. Pick each unit (friendly and enemy) within 9" of it to be the targets.
abilities.effect: Roll a dice for each target. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll. Then, this **MANIFESTATION** is destroyed.

abilities.name: Necrotic Vortex
abilities.effect:
Give this **MANIFESTATION** 1 maelstrom point each time:

• This **MANIFESTATION** is set up.
• A unit successfully casts a spell while it is within 12" of this **MANIFESTATION**.
• A model is slain within 12" of this **MANIFESTATION**.

This **MANIFESTATION** can have a maximum of **6 maelstrom points** at once

abilities.name: Traverse the Tides of Death
abilities.declare: Pick a friendly **INFANTRY WIZARD HERO** within 3" of this **MANIFESTATION** to be the target.
abilities.effect: This **MANIFESTATION** can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. It can pass through models during that move but cannot end that move in combat. Then, remove the target from the battlefield and set them up again on the battlefield within 3" of this **MANIFESTATION** and not in combat. Then, inflict 1 mortal damage on the target.

abilities.name: Pulled to the Grave
abilities.effect: This **MANIFESTATION** can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. It can pass through models during that move but cannot end that move in combat. Then, you can pick an enemy unit that this **MANIFESTATION** passed across during that move to be the target. Roll a dice for each model in the target unit. For each 5+, inflict 1 mortal damage on the target.

abilities.name: End Given Form
abilities.effect: This **MANIFESTATION** can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. It can pass through models during that move but cannot end that move in combat. Then, pick up to 3 enemy units that this **MANIFESTATION** passed across during that move to be the targets. Roll a D3 for each target. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.

abilities.name: Pull of the Nadir
abilities.effect: Subtract 1 from save rolls for friendly and enemy units while they are within 3" of this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Blinding Light
abilities.effect: This **MANIFESTATION** cannot be targeted by shooting attacks. In addition, a unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks if it is impossible to draw a straight line from a model in the attacking unit to a model in the target unit without that line passing across this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Bounteous Healing
abilities.declare: Pick a friendly unit within 3" of this **MANIFESTATION** to be the target.
abilities.effect: **Heal (3)** the target.

abilities.name: Restored Vigour
abilities.effect: **Heal (3)** this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Ensnaring Soul-Drain
abilities.effect: Each time an enemy unit uses a **MOVE** ability while it is within 9" of any parts of this **MANIFESTATION**, the effects of the ‘Fly’ ability do not apply to that unit. Enemy units cannot be set up within 9" of either part of this **MANIFESTATION**.

abilities.name: Phantasmal Translocation
abilities.effect: Pick a part of this **MANIFESTATION**, remove it from the battlefield and set it up again on the battlefield wholly within 9" of the other part and more than 3" from all enemy units.

abilities.name: Multiple Parts
abilities.effect: When a number of damage points equal to this **MANIFESTATION**’s Health characteristic are allocated to it, this **MANIFESTATION** is destroyed and both its parts are removed from play.

abilities.name: Bound for the Great Oubliette
abilities.declare: For each part of this **MANIFESTATION**, you can pick an enemy unit within 3" of that part to be the targets. You cannot pick the same unit to be the target of this ability more than once per turn.
abilities.effect:
Roll a D3 for each target. On a 2+:

• Inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.
• Subtract a number of inches equal to the roll from the target’s Move characteristic for the rest of the turn.

abilities.name: Multiple Parts
abilities.effect: When a number of damage points equal to this **MANIFESTATION**’s Health characteristic are allocated to it, this **MANIFESTATION** is destroyed and all its parts are removed from play.

abilities.name: Ravening Hunger
abilities.declare: If this **MANIFESTATION** charged this turn, pick an enemy unit within 1" of it to be the target.
abilities.effect: Roll 10 dice. For each 5+, inflict 1 mortal damage on the target.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Big Drogg Fort-Kicka
regimentsOfRenown.lore:
Of all of Behemat’s spawn, Gatebreakers are the most sinister, filled with the need to destroy. More specifically, these gargants harbour considerable ill will towards the metropolises raised by Sigmar’s folk. The sight of a glorious city, with its proud spires and resolute walls, awakens a bitter fury within these creatures. With a bellow of hate, a Gatebreaker advances on an enemy settlement without fear, shrugging off panicked arrows and cannon fire with little more than a malicious chuckle.

Across the Prime Innerlands of Shyish, the name Big Drogg Fort-kicka is dreaded. Admittedly, this is as much for the Gatebreaker’s grievous halitosis – a side effect of eating decomposing corpses – as his skill at destroying cities. Drogg is known to many undead tyrants, who willingly offer him a portion of their servants to consume in return for his aid. Yet Drogg’s true goal has always been to get close enough to Nagash to clobber him in a show of strength. The Great Necromancer’s shattering at the hands of the Lumineth has set this plan back. Taking this as a personal insult, Drogg has vowed revenge. He eagerly hires his services out to any undead wishing to fight the aelves – and he has no shortage of clients.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Big Grikk's Kruleshots
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Grikk swears that the massive, jagged bolt that buried itself in the back of his boss’s Vulcha steed had nothing to do with him. Few believed the treacherous Big Shot, so Grikk quickly took leave of his clan, accompanied by a handful of crossbow-toting lackeys and some grots to drag his beloved killbow across the realms.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Blades of the Hollow King
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Cado Ezechiar is an outcast and exile, and his destiny leads him on a path drenched with blood. Two companions accompany him on this dark odyssey. The first is the vampire warrior Sissendra, a ruthless and graceful killer who shares none of Cado’s self-restraint. The second is the Nulahmian death-priest Aurelias, a trusted servant of her majesty Neferata. There are few foes this trio cannot overcome.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Bloodthirsty Shiver
regimentsOfRenown.lore: The Idoneth refer to a formation of two Allopexes as a Bloodthirsty Shiver – and for good reason. Perfected by the warlike Fuethán enclave and sought after by many forces of Order, this deadly pairing is fast, resilient and brimming with raw killing power. In their wake, the ethersea is stained red with the blood of the foe.

The Rabble-Rowza and his mates (not to mention their squiggly companions) are an unpredictable menace, arriving without warning in search of shiny bottles to snatch and pour their fungal potions into.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Brand's Oathbound
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Gunnar Brand is a legend amongst the Darkoath tribes of the Great Parch in Aqshy. He is a champion of the gods, however unwillingly, and though often found leading his tribe to war, there are times when he must roam far from his homeland. With him come his keen-eyed daughter Singri, the muscular huscarl Dendrel, the twisted shaman Nadja, and Tanari of the Takblood, both a rival and a staunch ally.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Bundo Whalebiter
regimentsOfRenown.lore:
Kraken-eater Mega-Gargants are irascible old souls who have seen it all. They find just as much satisfaction in watching the sun rise while seated atop their favourite rock as they do in destroying a village, and they crave the relief that only the sea can provide – as well as the bounty that washes up on its shores.

Bundo Whalebiter is a legend along Ghur’s Coast of Tusks. He is that rarest of things, a gargant known for both brain and brawn, and has been ever since an encounter with ocean-dwelling aelves near the Icefangs. While pursuing the aelves – for the sight of their sea creatures flying about out of the water irritated him to no end – Bundo was distracted by a Fangmora Eel to which a glowing lantern had been attached. The gargant throttled the creature and took the trinket, hanging it from his ear as a fetching accoutrement. The aetherquartz in the lantern worked its magics upon Bundo, heightening his intellect such that he can now converse with shocking clarity for a gargant. As a result, Bundo has won many mercenary contracts by arguing the value in having colossal aid to call upon.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Casket of Resurrections
regimentsOfRenown.lore: The dark pacts sworn to ascertain the immortal power of a Black Coach are only spoken of in whispers, for these maligned conveyances are potent in the essence of Shyish. Deathly lords vanquished by their enemies know that banishment to the underworlds is but an ephemeral inconvenience should their minions manage to align themselves with one of these revivifying carriages of the night.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Cogfort Raiders
regimentsOfRenown.lore: The Conqueror Cogfort’s fire-spewing power and ability to transport a hold full of soldiers into battle makes it well suited for parties of raiders and pillagers. Rumours spread of these titans roaming the realms, their crews searching for plunder. Many of them are said to have fallen into the clutches of humanity’s foes.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Craventhrone Executioners
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Without hesitation will Kurdoss Valentian send out his vassals in service of his deathly allies. For the Craven King, it allows some semblance of the agency otherwise denied him, as well as the grim satisfaction that his every deed is dictated by his queen. Such is their disdain for their liege-lord that these cruel-hearted executioners will readily lend their bows to the highest bidder, as mercenary in death as they were in life.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Da Hurtlin' Hogz
regimentsOfRenown.lore: No one can now say whether Zog or Goz kicked off their stampeding race. The former claims to have been the boss, with Goz challenging him for primacy. The truth is anyone’s guess. The two charge and brawl across hill, valley and realmgate, driving their Maw-gruntas on. Any onlookers who tried to keep up have long since fallen behind, but sometimes da Hurtlin’ Hogz will crash through a battlefield. The cries of trampled enemies and barbarous whoops of nominal allies are ignored. Only pulling ahead matters.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Da Kountin' Krew
regimentsOfRenown.lore: When Swampboss Skumdrekk – wily overseer of the Murkvast Menagerie – needs to make an example of some git, he rustles up his Kountin’ Krew. These hobgrots have earned a nasty reputation chasing down those who renege on their debts to da boss, pelting them with explosive, shrapnel-laden grenades. Of course, Skumdrekk and his boyz aren’t above taking on opportunistic mercenary work from those who can afford it. After all, the chaos of battle gives them a fine chance to sneak up on the target they’re pursuing.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Dawnrider Lance
regimentsOfRenown.lore: When the need is great, a Vanari Lord Regent may lead a squadron of Dawnriders to aid the efforts of the Lumineth’s allies. Since the Reinvention, the aelves of Hysh have come to understand that no matter their people’s great power, the realms would have been entirely consumed by Chaos if not for the efforts of other civilisations. Therefore, when the hour is dire, they will ride out to tip the scales in Order’s favour.

These rampant and rambunctious children of the Plague God are filled with unfettered abandon. Spreading filth and contamination with their antics, they are the Grandfather’s grotesque hunting hounds.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Odo Godswallow
regimentsOfRenown.lore:
Beast-smashers are considered, even by other gargants, to be a touch mad. In truth, they are more susceptible to the energy of the Waaagh! than most of their kin. It stains their vision green and opens their souls to a rough elemental power, leading them to believe that they can hear Gorkamorka talking to them – or even that they are the god himself. Either way, their goal is the same: to slaughter great beasts, just as Gorkamorka once did.

Odo Godswallow truly believes that he is Gorkamorka reborn. The energy of Kragnos’s return stirred him to a rampage, seeing him slaughter almost every monstrous beast along the Godswallow Delta. The shamanistic orruks that called the delta their home detected the touch of their god upon Odo and commanded their followers to mark his flesh with whirling green tattoos, the paint made from a mixture of beast blood, squig innards and crushed realmstone. The sacred warpaint seems to protect him just as it does the orruks. His legend has spread across Gallet; warclans travel for leagues to witness Da Living God in action, and Odo rarely fails to meet their barbarous expectations.

regimentsOfRenown.name: One-eyed Grunnock
regimentsOfRenown.lore:
It does not matter to the Warstompers who is fighting or why; all they care about is finding a vicious scrap to lose themselves in. The charge of a Warstomper is akin to being hit by a roaring Chamonic steam-engine, and then the titan begins swinging their huge club, ploughing through enemy ranks with howls of battle‑joy.

One-eyed Grunnock is truly infamous. Gargants far and wide tell of how he terrorised Ghurish Necrosia, battling the Ossiarch Bonereapers. To the Warstomper, the undead are perfect enemies that constantly attract new foes. He has violently thwarted the Bonereapers’ negotiations with other races on several occasions, most notably interrupting the signing of a treaty with a duardin fleet. Being pitched off the side of a mountain and forever burdened with a splitting headache was, admittedly, not part of Grunnock’s plan, but the chance to ensure that the fighting between the two forces would never end was worth it.

For a time, Grunnock relentlessly hunted Kragnos, fearing that the Drogrukh deity would drive his undead prey from Ghur. Yet now Kragnos rampages in Ghyran, leaving Grunnock free to crush his favoured enemies once more.

regimentsOfRenown.lore: None are more dedicated to recovering Hashut’s relics than Thorzbuul the Seeker. This fanatical priest has travelled far, hunting scraps of a Zharralid dirge said to be able to break the realms in twain. Accompanied by a hulking daemon-engine guardian – a gift from Sharnok of Ur-Zorn, following some mysterious service – Thorzbuul pursues his prize by ripping open ley lines and listening for fractious notes of dissonance in their echo. More than once has his quest seen him fight alongside other marauding hosts of Chaos.

The Seekers of Silver are airborne specialists sent by the Change God to recover what is rightfully his – and to dole out terrible punishments to those who would lay their hands upon his treasures.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Sigil Convocation
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Some Scinari Calligraves embark on the path of the wanderer, not content to restrict their efforts at healing the lands to Hysh. These aelves have mastered the art of magical coalescence, giving a degree of permanence to their spells, which they utilise to aid the allies of the Lumineth in other realms. The assemblages of magic summoned and shepherded by these roaming mage-scribes are known as Sigil Convocations.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Skulkrik's Loonladz
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Loonboss Skulkrik fought under the Bad Moon, once. He has sought to do so again ever since, its light sending him and his ladz into a wild frenzy in violent cahoots. These Moonclan grots are always on the move, hunting after rumours of the Bad Moon’s last sighting, and they will often appear on a battlefield unannounced should their lunar deity bless it with an appearance.

The elite groups known as Sky-Port Profiteers are dispatched to seek allies and secure the most vital territories. Litigious Codewrights negotiate terms, ably backed up by the firepower of their Grundstok bodyguards.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Snerk's Trogg-Fer-Hire
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Having learned the Great Secret of mercenary work from a local Mega-Gargant, Snerk Mooneata decided to give it a go. Finding little success, the bossgrot took the next best option: periodically waking a surly troggoth and acting as the go-between for the beast. Snerk, of course, holds onto the troggoth’s half of the pay. For safekeeping.

Though he has since transformed into a monstrous Varghulf, flashes of loyalty to his old master occasionally surface – so potent that they also seize the ghoulish ‘knights’ who still serve him.

Staggering across the land in a drunken stupor, these gargants just turn up on one side of the battlefield and canny commanders simply move their troops out of the way...

regimentsOfRenown.lore: Folklore swirls around the Many Mirrors of Vennatan, the greatest creations of that unfortunate sorcerer-artisan. The fourth such of his follies, the Accursed Reflection, is rumoured to have been glimpsed on battlefields all across the Mortal Realms. Its presence is never requested, yet arrive it does, preying upon magic and unleashing a parade of maniac manifestations of its own.

regimentsOfRenown.name: The Beast of Castle Sternieste
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Dreadful are the stories of the death-titan that makes its lair atop the spires of Castle Sternieste, a draconic fiend enslaved to the will of Mannfred von Carstein. When the Beast of Sternieste flies forth to prey upon the living, it does so with the blessings of its master. It will fight alongside any of Nagash’s hosts, so long as it can fill its gullet with warm flesh and rend terrified mortals with its monstrous talons.

regimentsOfRenown.name: The Blacktalons
regimentsOfRenown.lore: From scions of the Dark Gods to marauding orruk warlords, threats to Sigmar’s people lurk in every corner of the realms. It is Neave Blacktalon, the God-King’s foremost assassin, who must put down these fell champions. Neave travels upon winds of magic during battle to unleash storms of never-ending axe blows. With her come the Blacktalons, tightly knit companions and warriors of deadly renown all.

They take their foes apart with cruel pleasure, causing even their allies to look upon them with a mix of awe and fear.

regimentsOfRenown.name: The Curse-Steel Battery
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Many Daemonsmiths detest walking the muck of the realms, leaving underlings to handle direct trade. Yet Gurok the Leveller prefers the personal touch, as he believes only his magics can coax out the full devastating potential of his glorious engines. Even the dreaded Korghos Khul once sought the service of Gurok’s artillery, for it can break an enemy army’s spine in moments. Of course, Gurok is no fool. Once his weapons’ power has been aptly demonstrated, negotiations can begin in earnest…

Known as the Horizon Seekers, his retinue are often seconded to Sigmar's allies, serving as scouts and putting their hard-earned knowledge of the realms' hinterlands to good effect as they harry enemies and seize control of vital objectives.

regimentsOfRenown.lore: The monstrous spawn of Morbheg lurk wherever light cannot reach. One such place is the ruin of Hallow’s Watch, deep in the Nightlands of Shyish. Here, a most ravenous Terrorgheist lurks, devouring any who try to bind it. The tunnels under Hallow’s Watch are strange and, under the right moon, lead to many places across the realms. On those fell nights, the beast crawls out from the darkness, screaming its hunger…

They hear only the mournful cries of wayward souls, which has driven them to cruelty and madness. Followed by the chilling fogs of their homeland, these Outcasts follow the dead and the half-souled, hoping to vent their own bitterness.

Accompanied by a looming Gnarlmaw, they seed the earth with abundant contagion all while cataloguing the many poxes unleashed by their diligent efforts.

Such is the belief of Grand Justice Gormayne, who can be found travelling the realms with his pack of ‘jurors’. Those who understand the nature of the ghouls’ affliction may play along, taking advantage of the chance to direct their frenzy in battle. Plus, Gormayne can be surprisingly reasonable – at least some of the time…

He will send the Shinestealaz into battle with other children of Gorkamorka in order to reap the spoils of the biggest battles. Their help is rarely refused, for this pack of stabby, loot-hungry grots are vicious in the extreme.

Swirling around her ethereal majesty come her handmaidens, bound to share their lady’s torment and wail their soul-freezing wails for all eternity. Those mortals they set upon have their hearts seized by terror, moments before ghostly blades are driven into them.

Yet the Mortarch of Night can tip the balance of an engagement, both through personal might and by leading bloodthirsty Fell Bats and the wights of his own Castle Sternieste in devious ambushes. Lending his lance to Sigmar's allies in the fight against darkness, he is accompanied in battle by his loyal bodyguard Ortana. The two have developed a formidable synergy, timing their aerial charges to deliver a devastating one-two punch to the enemy.

regimentsOfRenown.name: Volt-Klaw's Enginecoven
regimentsOfRenown.lore: Not all Warlocks stay with their clan. Some chafe under authority; others flee after some contraption goes awry. It is not clear which category Grisk Volt-Klaw falls under, but the Galvaneer avoids wider Skavendom, hiring out his arsenal in exchange for exorbitant warpstone tithes - in part because of greed and in part because he has modified his creations to such extremes that they consume arcane fuel at an astonishing rate. With warpstone deposits forming across the realms in the Hour of Ruin, business is booming.

armiesOfRenown.name: Aelementiri Conclave
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Aelementiri temples are focused on achieving oneness with the realms. They go to battle to protect the land and are often accompanied by the war-forms of elemental spirits, known as aelementors, who share their goal of vanquishing those who would bring corruption and ruin to nature.

When you pick the Lumineth Realm-lords faction for your army, you can choose for it to be an Aelementiri Conclave Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Lumineth Realm-lords faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Aelementiri Conclave. All units in your army gain the **AELEMENTIRI CONCLAVE** keyword.

rules.name: World Runes
rules.effect:
Add 1 **Alaithi, Rune of the Mountain** to your **library** for each friendly **ALARITH** unit on the battlefield.

Add 1 **Oreali, Rune of the Wind** to your **library** for each friendly **HURAKAN** unit on the battlefield.

Add 1 **Ydriliqi, Rune of the River** to your **library** for each friendly **YDRILAN** unit on the battlefield.

You can have a maximum of 6 of each type of rune in your **library**.

rules.name: Entreat Spirits
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Your Movement Phase
rules.declare: You can use this ability if you have 3 or more runes in your **library**. Pick a friendly non- **UNIQUE MONSTER** unit that has been destroyed to be the target.
rules.effect: Remove any 3 runes from your **library**. Then, set up a replacement unit identical to the target wholly within 6" of a terrain feature and more than 9" from all enemy units. Allocate 6 damage points to the replacement unit (ward rolls cannot be made for those damage points).

rules.name: Depict Rune
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle Round (Army), Start of Battle Round
rules.declare: You must use this ability.
rules.effect:
Remove all runes from your **battle scripture**. Then, pick any number of runes to remove from your **library** and depict them on your **battle scripture**. Runes removed from your library in this way are removed for the rest of the battle.

**Designer’s Note:** *Runes are used to activate powerful abilities on your units’ warscrolls.*

rules.name: The Realms Fight Back
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Combat Phase
rules.declare: Pick a terrain feature. Then, pick an enemy unit within 6" of that terrain feature to be the target.
rules.effect:
Roll a D3 and apply the corresponding effect for the rest of the turn.

**1.  Eruption of Earth:** Inflict 3 mortal damage on the target.
**2.  Howling Winds:** Subtract 5 from the target’s control score.
**3.  Flash Flood:** The target has **STRIKE-LAST**.

spells.name: Cycle Manifestation

Pick a friendly **AELEMENTIRI CONCLAVE WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick 1 of the friendly **MANIFESTATIONS** below that is not on the battlefield, then make a casting roll of 2D6.

• **Sanctum of Amyntok**
• **Hyshian Twinstones**
• **Rune of Petrification**
spells.effect: If there is already a friendly **MANIFESTATION** from the list above on the battlefield, it is immediately banished and removed from play. Then, set up the **MANIFESTATION** you picked within 1" of the caster and visible to them. If that **MANIFESTATION** is a **Sanctum of Amyntok**, it must instead be set up wholly within 12" of the caster, more than 3" from all enemy units, and with its 3 parts touching each other so that they form a ring.

armiesOfRenown.name: Allies of the Free Cities
armiesOfRenown.lore:
The free cities of the God-King stand as bulwarks against the brutality of the daemon, the gheist and the orruk. Founded in an era of rekindled hope, these vast, fortified strongholds are interspersed throughout the Mortal Realms, each home to a bustling population of faithful citizens.

When you pick the Cities of Sigmar faction for your army, you can choose for it to be an Allies of the Free Cities Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Cities of Sigmar faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Allies of the Free Cities. All units in your army gain the **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** keyword.

rules.name: Form up, Allies!
rules.declare:
Pick a friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** unit to use this ability, then pick 2 other visible friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** units wholly within 12" of it to be the targets.

The unit using this ability and the targets:
• cannot be **under orders**.
• must each be a different unit type.
rules.effect:
For the rest of the battle round, the unit using this ability and the targets are **under orders**.

**Designer’s Note:** *‘Unit type’ refers to the name of the warscroll (Core Rules, 4.0), e.g. ‘Freeguild Cavalier-Marshal’ or ‘Allies of the Free Cities Auric Hearthguard’.*

rules.name: Forces of Order
rules.effect:
While 3 friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** units are wholly within 12" of each other and 1 of those units has the **AELF** keyword, 1 of those units has the **DUARDIN** keyword and 1 of those units has the **SIGMARITE** keyword:

• Those units have **WARD (5+)** against shooting attacks.
• Add 3 to those units’ control scores.
• Add 1 to wound rolls for those units’ combat attacks.

artefacts.name: Signet of the City
artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), Any Hero Phase
artefacts.declare: Pick this unit and each visible friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** unit wholly within 18" of this unit to be the targets.
artefacts.effect:
For the rest of the turn:

• The targets are **under orders**.
• The targets have **WARD (6+)**.
• The targets have a maximum control score of 1.

heroicTraits.name: Respected Commander
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Reaction: You declared the ‘Redeploy’ command for a visible **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** unit wholly within 18" of this unit
heroicTraits.effect: If you roll a 1-3 when determining the distance that unit can move, you can use a value of 4 instead.

lores.name: Allies of the Free Cities Spell Lore

spells.name: These Realms are Ours
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick an objective within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: For the rest of the turn, melee weapons used by friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** units have **Charge (+1 Damage)** for attacks that target enemy units that are contesting the target objective.

lores.name: Allies of the Free Cities Prayer Lore

spells.name: Divine Mandate
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick a visible friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: If the chanting roll was 10+, you can pick another eligible unit to be a second target. Until the start of your next turn, enemy units have a maximum control score of 1 while they are in combat with any of the targets.

armiesOfRenown.name: Barrow Legion
armiesOfRenown.lore:
The barrow kingdoms of the Deathrattle represent undeath in all its sterile glory. Echoes of a glorious past, these civilisations exist in a constant state of atrophy, awaiting the summons of a Wight King master to fight in another of their endless wars.

If one wished to glimpse into the future that Nagash intends for the Chaos-tormented lands of the realms, they would need look no further than the kingdoms of the Deathrattle. Once, these domains obeyed the rigid structures of feudal order, with thousands of peasants living out their harsh and short-lived existences in farms and guildhalls in service of their regent. In death, that order has remained intact, though all meaning and purpose beyond eternal service has long been lost. Skeletal fingers rake the dirt of barren fields, grasping not for tubers but for decaying corpses. Millstones grind on unceasingly, crushing bones to dust. And while this macabre mockery of feudal existence continues, the Wight King and his inner circle plot their next conquest.

When you pick the Soulblight Gravelords faction for your army, you can choose it to be a Barrow Legion Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules as described in Army of Renown: Barrow Legion instead of the Soulblight Gravelords faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Barrow Legion. All units in your army gain the Barrow Legion keyword. Wight King and Wight King on Skeletal Steed units in your army gain Warmaster.

rules.name: All to Dust
rules.effect: In the third and subsequent battle rounds, while a friendly **Cursed Sepulchre** is on the battlefield, subtract 1 from save rolls for enemy units.

rules.name: Grave Sentinels
rules.effect: The melee weapons used by friendly **BARROW LEGION** units have **Anti-charge (+1 Rend)** while they are wholly within 9" of a friendly **Cursed Sepulchre**.

rules.name: Endless Legions of Bone
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle Round (Army), Any Movement Phase
rules.declare: Pick a friendly non-**UNIQUE BARROW LEGION** unit that has been destroyed to be the target.
rules.effect:
Set up a replacement unit with half the number of models from the target unit (rounding up) wholly within 12" of a friendly **BARROW LEGION HERO** or wholly within 6" of a friendly **Cursed Sepulchre**. It must be set up more than 9" from all enemy units.

If it is your movement phase, you can set up the replacement unit more than 3" from all enemy units instead of more than 9", but if you set it up within 9" of any enemy units, it cannot use **CHARGE** abilities this turn.

rules.name: Gifts to the Petty Kingdoms
rules.declare: Pick up to 2 friendly non-**UNIQUE BARROW LEGION HEROES** that do not have an artefact of power to be the targets (you can pick units in reserve).
rules.effect:
Give each target 1 artefact of power from the ‘Barrow Legion’ Army of Renown rules.

**Designer’s Note**: *These artefacts of power are given to the* **HEROES** *during the deployment phase, not during army composition.*

rules.name: The Royal Crypt
rules.declare: This ability must be used instead of the ‘Deploy Faction Terrain Feature’ ability.
rules.effect: Set up 3 **Cursed Sepulchres** wholly within friendly territory, each within ½" of any of the others and more than 3" from all objectives and other terrain features. After you have done so, they have been deployed and they are considered to be a single **Cursed Sepulchre** terrain feature that has a Health characteristic of 24 instead of 8.

artefacts.name: Amulet of Nightmares
artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Any Combat Phase
artefacts.declare: Pick an enemy unit within 9" of this unit to be the target.
artefacts.effect: Roll a dice. On a 2+, the target has **STRIKE‑LAST** this phase.

artefacts.name: Grave-sand Pendant
artefacts.effect: If this unit would be destroyed, before removing it from play, roll a dice. On a 3+, this unit is not destroyed and any remaining damage points inflicted on it have no effect. Then, **Heal (1)** this unit. This unit cannot use this ability again for the rest of the battle.

artefacts.name: Ring of Stricken Souls
artefacts.effect: This unit has **WARD (5+)**.

artefacts.name: Crypt Dagger
artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, End of Any Turn
artefacts.declare: Pick an enemy **HERO** in combat with this unit to be the target.
artefacts.effect: Inflict D6 mortal damage on the target.

artefacts.name: Crown of Cold Command
artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn, Any Hero Phase
artefacts.declare: Pick a friendly **BARROW LEGION** unit wholly within 12" of this unit to be the target.
artefacts.effect:
Roll a dice. On a 3+:

• If the target is damaged, **Heal (3)** the target.
•  If the target is not damaged, return a number of slain models to it with a combined Health characteristic of up to 3.

heroicTraits.name: Spirit-eater
heroicTraits.effect: If any models were slain by your general this turn, **Heal (D6)** your general.

heroicTraits.name: Lord of the Arcane Aegis
heroicTraits.effect:
Pick 1 of the following keywords:

• **INFANTRY**
• **CAVALRY**
• **MONSTER**
• **BEAST**

For the rest of the battle, each time an attack targets this unit, if the attacking unit has the keyword you picked, an unmodified save roll of 4+ for that attack is always successful.

heroicTraits.name: Restless Tyrant
heroicTraits.declare: Pick a friendly **BARROW LEGION** unit wholly within 12" of this unit to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect: Add 2" to the target’s Move characteristic for the rest of the turn.

armiesOfRenown.name: Big Waaagh!
armiesOfRenown.lore:
To orruks, the Waaagh! is central to all things. It is a term that holds many meanings, but at its core, it is the energy that fills them in a fight – and the power that connects them to their almighty god, Gorkamorka. Everything that the orruks are is embodied in the Waaagh!. It is their battle cry – only ever voiced at the top of their lungs. It is the name given to their migratory invasions and wars of vandalism and slaughter. Perhaps most infamously, it is also a strange magic that belongs to them alone, escalating the harder they battle and bolstering their might – or, in the case of the Kruleboyz, their low cunning.

No orruk doubts that the Waaagh! exists and that traces of its power thrum in every rock, river and tree. This rough but ever-present spirituality means that many orruks are superstitious, venerating strange cults and local deities. Usually, these are distorted recollections of former bosses or exaggerations of some beast that gave them a good walloping when they tried hunting it. But above all these regional idols, every orruk – and hobgrot, though they are less honest about it – honours Gorkamorka, the two-headed god of the untamed wilds, who is formed in the beastly image of Da Boyz.

When you pick the Ironjawz or Kruleboyz faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Big Waaagh! Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules as described in Army of Renown: Big Waaagh! instead of your chosen faction rules. For each regiment you include in a Big Waaagh! Army of Renown that is led by an Ironjawz Hero, you must include a regiment that is led by a Kruleboyz Hero and vice versa. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options as described in Army of Renown: Big Waaagh!. All units in your army gain the Big Waaagh! keyword.

rules.name: Rally The Warclans
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn, Your Movement Phase
rules.declare: If your general is on the battlefield, pick a friendly **BIG WAAAGH!** unit that started the battle with 3 or more models and that has been destroyed to be the target.
rules.effect: Set up a replacement unit with half the number of models from the target unit (rounding up) wholly within 12" of your general and more than 9" from all enemy units.

rules.name: The Power of The Waaagh!
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn, Your Hero Phase
rules.declare: Pick up to 1 friendly **BIG WAAAGH! IRONJAWZ** unit and up to 1 friendly **BIG WAAAGH! KRULEBOYZ** unit to be the targets.
rules.effect: The targets have the **POWER OF THE WAAAGH!** keyword for the rest of the battle round.

rules.name: Notorious Bosses
rules.declare: Pick up to 1 friendly non-**UNIQUE BIG WAAAGH! IRONJAWZ HERO** and up to 1 friendly non‑**UNIQUE BIG WAAAGH! KRULEBOYZ HERO** that do not have a heroic trait to be the targets.
rules.effect:
Give each target 1 heroic trait from the Big Waaagh! heroic traits.

**Designer’s Note**: *These heroic traits are given to the targets during the deployment phase, not during army composition.*

rules.name: Possessed By The Power Of The Waaagh!
rules.effect:
While a friendly unit has the **POWER OF THE WAAAGH!** keyword:

• If that unit has the **IRONJAWZ** keyword, add 1 to run rolls and charge rolls for that unit.

• If that unit has the **KRULEBOYZ** keyword, that unit’s attacks score critical hits on unmodified hit rolls of 5+.

artefacts.name: Da Sneaky Stab-slab
artefacts.effect: Each time you make an unmodified save roll of 6 for a combat attack that targets this unit, inflict 1 mortal damage on the attacking unit after the **FIGHT** ability has been resolved.

heroicTraits.name: A Proper Sneak
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Your Hero Phase
heroicTraits.declare: You can use this ability if this unit has the **POWER OF THE WAAAGH!** keyword and a friendly unit has not used a **BRUTAL KUNNIN’** ability this turn.
heroicTraits.effect: For the rest of the battle round, friendly **BIG WAAAGH!** units have **WARD (5+)** while they are wholly within 12" of this unit.

heroicTraits.name: Get Krumpin'!
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Your Hero Phase
heroicTraits.declare: You can use this ability if this unit has the **POWER OF THE WAAAGH!** keyword and a friendly unit has not used a **BRUTAL KUNNIN’** ability this turn.
heroicTraits.effect: For the rest of the battle round, add 1 to wound rolls for combat attacks made by friendly **BIG WAAAGH!** units while they are wholly within 12" of this unit.

heroicTraits.name: Da Old One-Two
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Your Hero Phase
heroicTraits.declare: You can use this ability if this unit has the **POWER OF THE WAAAGH!** keyword and a friendly unit has not used a **BRUTAL KUNNIN’** ability this turn.
heroicTraits.effect: For the rest of the battle round, friendly **BIG WAAAGH!** units’ melee weapons have **CRIT (2 HITS)** while they are wholly within 12" of this unit.

heroicTraits.name: Takin' Names
heroicTraits.effect: If an enemy **HERO** is destroyed by this unit, this unit has the **POWER OF THE WAAAGH!** keyword for the rest of the battle.

spells.name: Two Headz As One
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **BIG WAAAGH! WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **BIG WAAAGH! INFANTRY** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect:
If the target is an **IRONJAWZ** unit, until the start of your next turn, add 3 to the target’s control score while it is wholly within 12" of any friendly **KRULEBOYZ** units.

If the target is a **KRULEBOYZ** unit, until the start of your next turn, add 2" to the target’s Move characteristic while it is wholly within 12" of any friendly **IRONJAWZ** units.

spells.name: Unstoppable Waaagh!-beats
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **BIG WAAAGH! PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick a visible friendly non-**HERO BIG WAAAGH! INFANTRY** unit that is in combat and wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: The target can use 2 **FIGHT** abilities this turn. After the first is used, however, the target has **STRIKE‑LAST** for the rest of the turn. If the chanting roll was 10+, you can pick another eligible unit to be a second target.

spells.name: Summon Foot of Gork
spells.declare: If there is not a friendly **Foot of Gork** on the battlefield, pick a friendly **BIG WAAAGH! WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Set up a **Foot of Gork** wholly within 12" of the caster, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units. A **Foot of Gork** has 2 parts that must be set up within 9" of each other.

unitPool: Scourge of Ghyran Killaboss on Great Gnashtoof

warscrolls: Scourge of Ghyran Killaboss on Great Gnashtoof

armiesOfRenown.name: Champions of the Arena
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Graceful, lethal and trained from birth in the arts of war, the aelves known as the Daughters of Khaine see bloodshed as a holy calling. Each throat they slash and each jetting arc of gore that issues forth is an act of worship, a prayer to the merciless god from whom they derive their name.

When you pick the Daughters of Khaine faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Champions of the Arena Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Daughters of Khaine faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Champions of the Arena. All units in your army gain the **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** keyword.

rules.name: Arena of Slaughter
rules.effect:
If the number of units (friendly or enemy) that have been destroyed this battle equals or exceeds any of the numbers below, the corresponding effect applies to friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** units for the rest of the battle. More than 1 effect can apply to a unit at the same time.

**1** Add 1 to run rolls for those units.
**2** Add 1 to charge rolls for those units.
**3**  Add 1 to hit rolls for those units’ combat attacks.
**4**  Add 1 to wound rolls for those units’ combat attacks.
**5**  Add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of those units’ melee weapons.

rules.name: Let the Blood Flow
rules.effect:
All effects of the ‘Arena of Slaughter’ ability apply to friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** units while they are wholly within **X**" of a friendly **Shrine of Dark Tribute**, where **X** is determined by the current battle round, as follows:

**Battle Round** - **X**
1 - 9"
2 - 12"
3 - 15"
4 - 18"
5 - 21"

rules.name: Frenzied Restoration
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **Champions of the Arena Hero** that has been destroyed to be the target.
rules.effect: Set up a replacement unit identical to the target wholly within 12" of a friendly **Shrine of Dark Tribute** and more than 9" from all enemy units.

rules.name: Explosive Fury
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Combat Phase
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **Slaughter Queen** to use this ability, then pick each friendly **Blood Hags** unit on the battlefield and each visible friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** unit wholly within 12" of that **Slaughter Queen** to be the targets.
rules.effect: For the rest of the turn, each time a target makes a pile-in move, add 3" to the distance it can move.

artefacts.name: Death Razor
artefacts.phaseDetails: Reaction: You declared a **FIGHT** ability for this unit
artefacts.effect: After that **FIGHT** ability has been resolved, if any enemy models were slain by this unit’s attacks made as part of that **FIGHT** ability, you can pick a friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA PRIEST** wholly within 12" of this unit to be the target. Instead of making a chanting roll for the next **PRAYER** ability used by the target, you can use a value of 10 for the roll that cannot be modified, even if this unit has been destroyed.

heroicTraits.name: Hero of the Killing Games
heroicTraits.effect:
For the rest of the turn, while this unit is in combat with any enemy **HEROES**:
• Add 1 to the Attacks, Rend and Damage characteristics of this unit’s melee weapons.
• All of its combat attacks must target the same enemy **HERO**.

lores.name: Champions of the Arena Prayer Lore

spells.name: Shield of Blood
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick a visible friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: If the chanting roll was 10+, you can pick up to 2 other eligible units to be additional targets. Each target has **WARD (5+)** until the start of your next turn.

spells.name: Divine Rush
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick a visible friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect:
If the chanting roll was 6+, you can pick another eligible unit to be a second target. For each target:

• If the target is not in combat, it can move D6". It cannot end that move in combat.
• If the target is in combat, it can make a pile‑in move.

spells.name: Call of the Arena
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA PRIEST** to chant this prayer, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: For the rest of the turn, add 1 to run rolls for friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** units while they are wholly within 12" of the chanter. In addition, if the chanting roll was 6+, for the rest of the turn, add 1 to charge rolls for friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA** units while they are wholly within 12" of the chanter.

manifestationLores.name: Champions of the Arena Manifestation Lore

spells.name: Violent Consecration
spells.declare:
Pick a friendly **CHAMPIONS OF THE ARENA PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick 1 of the friendly **MANIFESTATIONS** below that is not on the battlefield, then make a chanting roll of D6.

**• Bloodwrack Viper**
**• Bladewind**
**• Heart of Fury**
spells.effect:
If you picked a **Bloodwrack Viper** or **Bladewind**, set it up wholly within 12" of the chanter, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units. If you picked a **Heart of Fury**, set it up wholly within 18" of the chanter and visible to them.

If the chanting roll was 10+, you can pick a second **MANIFESTATION** from the list above that is not on the battlefield and set it up as described above.

armiesOfRenown.name: Change-Cult Uprising
armiesOfRenown.lore:
There are a thousand Arcanite cults across the Mortal Realms, ranging from a handful of hidden conspirators to entire civilian underclasses who simply await the command of their leaders to begin their long-awaited purge. The greatest of these secret collectives are overseen by charismatic masterminds chosen by the Change God for their cruel cunning and boundless ambition. These Fatemasters and Magisters hold the fates of thousands in their grasping hands. They have learnt from their master how best to exploit the weaknesses and sins of mortals in order to see Tzeentch's will carried out.

When you pick the Disciples of Tzeentch faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Change-cult Uprising Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Disciples of Tzeentch faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box as described in Army of Renown: Change-Cult Uprising. All units in your army gain the **CHANGE-CULT** keyword.

rules.name: Silver Simulacrum
rules.effect:
If there is a friendly **Argent Shard** on the battlefield, you can set up 1 additional **Argent Shard** on the battlefield. It must be set up wholly within friendly territory and more than 3" from all objectives and other terrain features.

**Designer’s Note:** *The original Argent Shard is set up in Step 1 of the deployment phase using the ‘Deploy Faction Terrain’ ability. This ability allows you to set up a second such terrain feature and is used in Step 3 of the deployment phase, after armies have been deployed.*

rules.name: Puppet on a String
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Hero Phase
rules.declare: Pick an enemy **HERO** within 24" of and visible to a friendly **CHANGE-CULT HERO** to be the target.
rules.effect:
For the rest of the turn, each time the target uses a **CORE** ability, gain 1 **fate point**.

**Designer’s Note:** *You can only gain fate points using this ability and the ‘Spell-eater Pendant’ ability.*

rules.name: Illusory Infiltration
rules.declare: Pick up to 3 friendly non-reinforced **CHANGE-CULT KAIRIC ACOLYTES** units to be the targets.
rules.effect: Remove the targets from the battlefield and set them up again wholly within 3" of a terrain feature and not in combat.

rules.name: Overtaken by Events
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Hero Phase
rules.declare: Spend 1 **fate point**. Then, pick an enemy unit within 24" of and visible to a friendly **CHANGE‑CULT HERO** to be the target.
rules.effect: The target cannot use commands for the rest of the turn.

rules.name: Seeds of Discord
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: Spend 1 **fate point**. Then, pick an enemy model within 24" of and visible to a friendly **CHANGE-CULT HERO** to be the target, then pick another enemy unit within the target’s combat range to be the victim.
rules.effect: Pick 1 of the target’s melee weapons. Immediately resolve combat attacks for the target made with that weapon against the victim.

artefacts.name: Spell-Eater Pendant
artefacts.effect: Each time this unit unbinds a spell or banishes an enemy **MANIFESTATION**, gain 1 **fate point**.

heroicTraits.name: Call of Anarchy
heroicTraits.declare: Pick a terrain feature within 18" of this unit, then pick a friendly non-**HERO CHANGE-CULT** unit that is **masked by illusion** or that has been destroyed to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect:
If you picked a unit that is **masked by illusion**, set it up wholly within 3" of that terrain feature and more than 6" from all enemy units.

If you picked a unit that has been destroyed, set up a replacement unit with half the number of models from the target unit (rounding up) wholly within 3" of that terrain feature and more than 6" from all enemy units.

**Designer’s Note:** *Units can be set up in reserve masked by illusion using the Fatemaster’s ‘Long in the Planning’ ability.*

spells.name: Eldritch Strength
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CHANGE-CULT WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **CHANGE-CULT** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Add 1 to wound rolls for the target’s attacks until the start of your next turn.

spells.name: Shifting Manifestations
spells.declare:
Pick a friendly **CHANGE-CULT WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick 1 of the friendly **MANIFESTATIONS** below that is not on the battlefield, then make a casting roll of 2D6.

**• Burning Sigil of Tzeentch**
**• Daemonic Simulacrum**
**• Tome of Eyes**
spells.effect: If there is already a friendly **MANIFESTATION** from the list above on the battlefield, it is immediately banished and removed from play. Then, set up the **MANIFESTATION** you picked within 1" of the caster and visible to them.

armiesOfRenown.name: Court of the Godlings
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Dexcessa is an arrogant force of nature who cannot abide taking orders from another. They see themselves as Slaanesh’s chief warlord, often overthrowing lesser champions and ordering their warhosts to abandon long-fought campaigns and focus on new, bizarre objectives more pleasing to the Talon. Only Synessa’s wicked tongue can convince Dexcessa to listen – and even then the daemon rails at the patience advised by their despised sibling, ever liable to suddenly act.
When you pick the Hedonites of Slaanesh faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Court of the Godlings Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules described in Army of Renown: Court of the Godlings instead of the Hedonites of Slaanesh faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box opposite. All units in your army gain the **COURT OF THE GODLINGS** keyword.

rules.name: The Voice and the Talon
rules.effect: The friendly **Synessa** and **Dexcessa** have the **TWIN** keyword.

rules.name: The More Glorious Slaughter
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn, Start of Any Turn
rules.declare: If a friendly **TWIN** destroyed an enemy unit in the previous turn and the other did not, pick the one that did not to use this ability.
rules.effect: For the rest of the turn, add 1 to hit rolls for combat attacks made by units in the regiment of the unit using this ability.

rules.name: The Longer Shadow
rules.declare: If a friendly **TWIN** is contesting an objective you control and the other is not, pick the one that is not to use this ability.
rules.effect: Until the start of your next turn, while the unit using this ability is contesting an objective you control, add 5 to the control scores of units in its regiment.

rules.name: The Grander Visage
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Hero Phase
rules.declare: If either friendly **TWIN** is damaged, pick the one with fewer or no damage points to use this ability.
rules.effect: For the rest of the turn, the unit using this ability has **WARD (4+)**.

rules.name: The Swifter Surge
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Charge Phase
rules.declare: If a friendly **TWIN** charged in the previous turn and the other did not, pick the one that did not to use this ability.
rules.effect: For the rest of the phase, when making charge rolls for units in the regiment of the unit using this ability, add 1 to the number of dice rolled, to a maximum of 3, then remove 1 dice of your choice and use the remaining dice as the charge roll.

rules.name: The More Cunning Ruse
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: If both friendly **TWINS** are within 12" of and visible to each other, pick one of them to use this ability.
rules.effect: Remove the unit using this ability from the battlefield and set it up on the battlefield more than 9" from all enemy units.

artefacts.name: The Triplet Trinket
artefacts.effect:
While a friendly **TWIN** is within 3" of and visible to this unit, subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target that **TWIN** and this unit.
While 2 friendly **TWINS** are within 3" of and visible to this unit, subtract 1 from hit rolls and wound rolls for attacks that target those **TWINS** and this unit instead.

heroicTraits.name: Tethered to the Twins
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Your Hero Phase
heroicTraits.declare: Pick a friendly **TWIN** that has been destroyed to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect: Set up a replacement unit identical to the target within 12" of this unit and more than 9" from all enemy units.

lores.name: Court of the Godlings Spell Lore

spells.name: Sibilant Whispers
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **COURT OF THE GODLINGS WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible enemy unit within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, while a friendly **TWIN** is visible to the target, halve the target’s Move characteristic. In addition, while 2 friendly **TWINS** are visible to the target, your opponent must roll 1 fewer dice, to a minimum of 1, when making charge rolls for it.

spells.name: Ecstatic Furore
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **COURT OF THE GODLINGS WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **TWIN** wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, add 1 to wound rolls for combat attacks made by friendly **COURT OF THE GODLINGS** units while they are wholly within 12" of and visible to the target.

spells.name: Essence Transfusion
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **COURT OF THE GODLINGS WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **TWIN** to be the donor, pick another visible friendly **TWIN** to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Allocate any number of damage points to the donor (ward rolls cannot be made for those damage points). Then, **Heal (X)** the target, where **X** is double the number of damage points allocated to the donor by this use of this ability.

manifestationLores.name: Court of the Godlings Manifestation Lore

spells.name: Summon Manifestation of Depravity
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **COURT OF THE GODLINGS WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect:
Pick a friendly **HEDONITES OF SLAANESH MANIFESTATION** that is not on the battlefield and that was not removed from play earlier in the same turn.

If you picked a **Dreadful Visage** or **Wheels of Excruciation**, set it up wholly within 12" of the caster, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units. If you picked a **Mesmerising Mirror**, set it up wholly within 12" of the caster and visible to them.

armiesOfRenown.name: Cycle of Corruption
armiesOfRenown.lore:
It is through his mortal Rotbringers that the Plague God first expands his influence. Their creed is to poison everything, thereby extending the borders of the Garden of Nurgle, the Grandfather’s domain in the Realm of Chaos. Crops are ruined, livestock sickened and settlements left decaying. From this foulness can new, horrific life grow, the land fertile with burgeoning rot. This is the cycle of corruption, an ever-churning gyre of collapse and grisly rebirth, through which Nurgle’s whispers can take root.

When you pick the Maggotkin of Nurgle faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Cycle of Corruption Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Maggotkin of Nurgle faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box as described in Army of Renown: Cycle of Corruption. All units in your army gain the **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION** keyword.

rules.name: Revolution and Revulsion
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle Round (Army), Start of Battle Round
rules.declare: You must use this ability at the start of each battle round.
rules.effect:
If it is the first battle round, roll a dice to determine which **CYCLE** ability is ripe for the rest of the battle round.

1 ‘Unnatural Vitality’
2 ‘Fecund Vigour’
3 ‘Burgeoning Filth’
4 ‘Plague of Misery’
5 ‘Nauseous Revulsion’
6 ‘Rampant Disease’
7 ‘Corrupted Regrowth’

If it is not the first battle round, the next **CYCLE** ability in the sequence is ripe for the rest of the battle round. If the ‘Corrupted Regrowth’ ability was ripe last battle round, the ‘Unnatural Vitality’ ability is ripe this battle round.

You can only use a **CYCLE** ability if it is ripe. Only 1 **CYCLE** ability is ripe each battle round.

rules.name: Unnatural Vitality
rules.effect: Friendly non-**MONSTER CYCLE OF CORRUPTION HEROES** have **WARD (4+)**.

rules.name: Fecund Vigour
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Your Hero Phase
rules.effect: **HEAL (D3)** each friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION** unit.

rules.name: Burgeoning Filth
rules.effect: Subtract 3 from the control scores of enemy units while they are in combat with any friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION** units.

rules.name: Plague of Misery
rules.effect: While enemy units are within 12" of any friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION** units, they cannot use abilities that heal or return slain models to a unit.

rules.name: Nauseous Revulsion
rules.effect: Subtract 1 from charge rolls for enemy units.

rules.name: Rampant Disease
rules.effect: Melee weapons used by friendly non-**HERO CYCLE OF CORRUPTION** units have **Crit (2 Hits)**.

rules.name: Corrupted Regrowth
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Your Hero Phase
rules.effect:
**Heal (7)** each friendly **Feculent Gnarlmaw**.

If there are no friendly **Feculent Gnarlmaws** on the battlefield, you can set up a **Feculent Gnarlmaw** on the battlefield more than 9" from all enemy units and more than 3" from all objectives and other terrain features.

artefacts.name: Cankerous Nail
artefacts.effect: Add 1 to the Rend characteristic of this unit’s melee weapons.

heroicTraits.name: Utterly Disgusting
heroicTraits.effect: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target this unit.

spells.name: Epidermal Crustitis
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, subtract 1 from the Damage characteristic of melee weapons used for attacks that target the caster.

spells.name: Rank and Vile
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible enemy **INFANTRY** unit within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, each time a model in the target unit is slain, after removing it from the battlefield, roll a dice. On a 4+, return 1 slain model to a friendly non-**HERO CYCLE OF CORRUPTION INFANTRY** unit within 6" of the target.

spells.name: Spittledrain
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible enemy unit within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Subtract 1 from the target’s Move characteristic until the start of your next turn. Then, roll a number of dice equal to the number of models in the target unit. For each 6, inflict 1 mortal damage on the target, to a maximum of 7.

spells.name: Virulent Agonies
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick a visible enemy unit within 18" of them to be the target, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: Inflict 1 mortal damage on the target. Then, inflict 1 mortal damage on each other enemy unit within the target’s combat range. If the chanting roll was 7+, inflict D3 mortal damage on the target instead of 1.

spells.name: Bacterial Blessing
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION PRIEST** to chant this prayer, pick a visible friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, add 1 to wound rolls for the target’s combat attacks. If the chanting roll was 7+, you can pick another eligible unit to be a second target.

spells.name: Ever Turning
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **CYCLE OF CORRUPTION PRIEST** to chant this prayer, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect: The next **CYCLE** ability in the sequence is ripe for the rest of the battle round instead of the current **CYCLE** ability. If the chanting roll was 12+, you can pick which **CYCLE** ability is ripe.

armiesOfRenown.name: Da King's Gitz
armiesOfRenown.lore:
By far the most successful Loonboss to have lived is Skragrott. This cunning wizard bears a fungal crown whose roots burrow into his brain, and a rare, crescent-shaped Bossfungus caps his staff – a clear sign of the Bad Moon’s favour. Though his followers are many, his closest allies are the skraps known as the King’s Gitz. These loyal Moonclanners enabled Skragrott to conquer Skrappa Spill, his personal gloomy paradise in the Realm of Metal. Emboldened by the centuries of success enjoyed by their master, they are considerably less cowardly than their peers and much more willing to throw themselves into the thick of battle. However, not even they are above the temptation of personal gain – in fact, they are amongst the most ambitious due to the power they wield as the biggest bullies around. The leaders of these skraps are constantly changing because of their habitual backstabbing.

When you pick the Gloomspite Gitz faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Da King’s Gitz Army of Renown. If you do so, use thosee faction rules instead of the Gloomspite Gitz faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box as described in Army of Renown: Da King's Gitz. All units in your army gain the King’s Gitz keyword.

rules.name: Da King's Guards
rules.effect:
While a friendly **TROGGOTH** unit is within the combat range of a friendly **HERO**:

• That **HERO** has **WARD (4+)**.
• Each time you make a successful ward roll for that **HERO**, allocate 1 damage point to a friendly **TROGGOTH** unit within that **HERO**’s combat range after the damage sequence for that **HERO** has been resolved (ward rolls cannot be made for those damage points).

rules.name: Da Loonking's Court
rules.declare: Pick up to D3 friendly non-**UNIQUE KING’S GITZ HEROES** that do not have an enhancement.
rules.effect:
Give each **HERO** 1 enhancement from the ‘Da King’s Gitz’ Army of Renown rules.

**Designer’s Note**: *These enhancements are given to the* **HEROES** *during the deployment phase, not during army composition.*

rules.name: The Moon and The Loon
rules.effect:
While a friendly unit is wholly within 9" of a friendly **Skragrott**, a friendly **Malevolent Moon** or a friendly **Bad Moon Loonshrine**:

•  Add 5 to the control scores of friendly non-**SQUIG MOONCLAN** units.
•  Instead of rolling the dice to determine the Move characteristic of a friendly **SQUIG** unit, you can use a value of 4.

artefacts.name: Loonstone Medallion
artefacts.effect: This unit has **WARD (6+)**. While this unit is contesting an objective that is not being contested by any other friendly units, it has **WARD (5+)**.

artefacts.name: Wotnot of Prestige
artefacts.declare: Pick a point on the battlefield within 9" of this unit. Each visible enemy unit within 3" of that point is a target.
artefacts.effect: Roll a D3 for each target. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.

artefacts.name: Moonhoned Shiv
artefacts.effect: Pick 1 of this unit’s non-**Companion** melee weapons. Add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of that weapon for the rest of the battle. In addition, each time this unit uses a **RETREAT** ability, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of that weapon for the rest of the battle. This unit can be affected by this ability multiple times and the effects are cumulative.

heroicTraits.name: Glarejester
heroicTraits.declare: Pick a friendly **INFANTRY** unit within 3" of this unit and that is not in combat to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect: Roll a D3. Add the roll to the distance the target can move when using **MOVE** abilities this phase. If the target ends a move in this phase within 3" of this **HERO**, inflict an amount of mortal damage on this **HERO** equal to the roll.

heroicTraits.name: Da King's Adjutant
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), End of Your Turn
heroicTraits.declare: You cannot use this ability and the **Bad Moon Loonshrine**’s ‘Moonclan Lairs’ ability in the same turn. If this unit is within 12" of a friendly **Bad Moon Loonshrine**, pick a friendly non-reinforced, non-**HERO**, non-**MONSTER KING’S GITZ** unit that has been destroyed to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect: Set up a replacement unit wholly within 12" of a friendly **Bad Moon Loonshrine** and more than 3" from all enemy units.

spells.name: Right Gitz In Da Right Place
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **KING’S GITZ WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly unit wholly within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: The target’s weapons have **Anti-HERO (+1 Rend)** until the start of your next turn.

spells.name: Da Loonking's Command
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **KING’S GITZ WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly unit wholly within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect:
Until the start of your next turn:

•  Add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of melee weapons used by champions in the target unit.
•  If the target uses the ‘Rally’ command, you can make 1 additional rally roll of D6.
•  Add 1 to the target’s control score.

spells.name: Summon Malevolent Moon
spells.declare: If there is not a friendly **Malevolent Moon** on the battlefield, pick a friendly **GLOOMSPITE GITZ WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Set up a **Malevolent Moon** wholly within 12" of the caster, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units.

armiesOfRenown.name: Decadent Host
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Sigvald’s sheer self-obsession is an elemental force that fills his followers with a need to offer him conquered lands as tribute. He delights in the adoration of ‘lesser’ mortals, and his volatile whim has spurred rampages across Ghur, the Ossiarch Empire and ravaged Hysh. Countless tales of atrocity surround the Decadent Host, and the Everchosen himself has called on them specifically to do his bidding. Sometimes, Sigvald even deigns to listen.

When you pick the Hedonites of Slaanesh faction for your army, you can choose for it to be the Decadent Host Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules described in Army of Renown the Decadent Host instead of the Hedonites of Slaanesh faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box opposite. All units in your army gain the **DECADENT HOST** keyword.

rules.name: Perfection Made Manifest
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Hero Phase
rules.declare: Pick the friendly **Sigvald** to use this ability then pick a visible unit (friendly or enemy) within 3" of him to be the target.
rules.effect: Inflict D3 mortal damage on the target. For each model slain by this ability this turn, add 2 to the Attacks characteristic of the friendly **Sigvald**’s melee weapons for the rest of the turn.

rules.name: The Prince in the Mirror
rules.declare: If the friendly **Sigvald** has been destroyed, pick him to be the target. He can be replaced any number of times.
rules.effect: Set up a replacement unit identical to the target wholly within 6" of a friendly **Contorted Epitome** and not in combat.

rules.name: Petulant Wrath
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Combat Phase
rules.declare: If the friendly **Sigvald** is not in combat and did not charge this turn, pick him to use this ability, then pick another friendly **DECADENT HOST** unit within 1" of him to be the target.
rules.effect: Inflict D6 mortal damage on the target. Before removing any slain models from play, remove the friendly **Sigvald** from the battlefield then set him up again within 1" of the target.

rules.name: Infernal Handmaidens
rules.effect: While the friendly **Sigvald** is within 3" of and visible to any friendly **DECADENT HOST DAEMON** units, add 1 to wound rolls for combat attacks made by those **DAEMON** units.

rules.name: All for the Prince
rules.effect:
While the friendly **Sigvald** is within the combat range of another friendly **DECADENT HOST SYBARITE** unit:

• He has **WARD (2+)**.
• Each time you make a successful ward roll for him, allocate 1 damage point to another friendly **DECADENT HOST** unit within his combat range after the damage sequence for him has been resolved (ward rolls cannot be made for those damage points).

rules.name: Important People Have Important Places To Be
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.effect: If the friendly **Sigvald** is not contesting an objective, he can move 2D6" but must end that move contesting an objective. He can move through the combat ranges of enemy units but cannot end that move in combat.

artefacts.name: Mirror-Plate
artefacts.effect:
Weapons used by this unit have a maximum Attacks characteristic of 1.

While this unit is within 3" of and visible to the friendly **Sigvald**, add 20 to the friendly **Sigvald**’s control score.

heroicTraits.name: Protégé
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), Reaction: You declared the 'The Prince in the Mirror' ability
heroicTraits.effect: You can set up a replacement **Sigvald** within 1" of this unit instead of within 6" of a friendly **Contorted Epitome**. If you do so, after setting up that replacement unit, this unit is automatically destroyed.

spells.name: Diverting Excruciation
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **DECADENT HOST WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **Sigvald** wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: **Heal (D3)** the target.

spells.name: Visions of Eternal Bliss
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **DECADENT HOST WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **Sigvald** wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, the target scores critical hits on unmodified hit rolls of 5+.

spells.name: Summon Manifestations of Depravity
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **DECADENT HOST WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect:
Pick a friendly **HEDONITES OF SLAANESH MANIFESTATION** that is not on the battlefield and that was not removed from play earlier in the same turn.

If you picked a **Dreadful Visage** or **Wheels of Excruciation**, set it up wholly within 12" of the caster, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units. If you picked a **Mesmerising Mirror**, set it up wholly within 12" of the caster and visible to them.

unitPool: Scourge of Ghyran Syll'Esske, the Vengeful Allegiance

armiesOfRenown.name: Draconith Skywing
armiesOfRenown.lore:
The union of Stormcast and Draconith is a mighty one. Its realms-changing potential is exemplified by the Draconith Skywing, devised by the legendary Ionus Crytborn. This elite formation is designed to overwhelm enemies from the sky, with dragon-riding Stormcasts descending like blazing spears to impale the enemy while their steeds spit streams of flame.
