armiesOfRenown.name: The Oracles of Fate
armiesOfRenown.lore:
The daemonic convocations of the Change God are a reflection of their master’s bizarre nature. These sorcerous armies are led by daemons for whom Tzeentch bears a particular fondness. Whether they be masters of intrigue, deranged fire-starters or mutators of living flesh, they all exist solely to bring about the Architect of Fate’s vision of endless, violent alteration.

When you pick the Disciples of Tzeentch faction for your army, you can choose for it to be the Oracles of Fate Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Disciples of Tzeentch faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box as described in Army of Renown: The Oracles of Fate All units in your army gain the **ORACLES OF FATE** keyword

rules.name: Masters of Destiny
rules.effect:
Roll 9 dice and place them to one side. These are your **destiny dice**. **Destiny dice** can be spent during the battle to change certain dice rolls. You can never have more than 9 **destiny dice** at once.

During the battle, instead of making a roll from the list below for a friendly **ORACLES OF FATE** unit, you can spend your **destiny dice**. The roll you would have made is replaced with the roll on the **destiny dice** you spend. If you want to replace a 2D6 roll, 3D6 roll, etc. you must spend a number of destiny dice equal to the number before ‘D6’. Any rolls that have been replaced count as unmodified rolls, and they cannot be re-rolled or modified by other friendly abilities except for those that apply negative modifiers (but they can be modified by enemy abilities). You cannot spend destiny dice to replace re-rolls.

**Destiny dice** can be spent in place of the following dice rolls:

• Casting rolls
• Unbinding rolls
• Banishment rolls
• Run rolls
• Charge rolls in your turn
• Hit rolls
• Wound rolls
• Save rolls

rules.name: Ninefold Blessings
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle Round, Start of Your Turn
rules.declare: You can only use this ability if the total value of your unspent **destiny dice** is exactly 9.
rules.effect:
Re-roll all of your unspent **destiny dice**. Then, until the start of your next turn:

• Friendly **ORACLES OF FATE** units have **WARD (5+)**.
• Subtract 1 from save rolls for enemy units.

**Designer’s Note:** *Add together the values shown on each unspent destiny dice to find the total value.*

artefacts.name: Corrupted Leystone
artefacts.effect: If this unit is contesting an objective, remove it from the battlefield and set it up again contesting an objective and more than 7" from all enemy units.

heroicTraits.name: Nexus of Fate
heroicTraits.effect: Roll a dice. If you have 9 unspent **destiny dice**, you must replace 1 of your **destiny dice** with that roll. Otherwise, place that dice to one side as a **destiny dice**.

lores.name: The Oracles of Fate Spell Lore

spells.name: Fateweaver's Gift
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ORACLES OF FATE WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect:
Pick 1 of the following effects:

***Change the Past:*** Pick an enemy unit within 12" of and visible to the caster, that is in combat and that has not been picked to be the target of this ability this turn to be the target. Roll either 4 dice or a number of dice equal to the number of unspent destiny dice you have. For each 4+, inflict 1 mortal damage on the target.
***Seize the Present:*** Until the start of your next turn, add 1 to run rolls and charge rolls for friendly **ORACLES OF FATE** units while they are wholly within 12" of the caster.
***Predict the Future:*** Roll a dice. You must replace 1 of your **destiny dice** with that roll.

manifestationLores.name: The Oracles of Fate Manifestation Lore

spells.name: Shifting Manifestations
spells.declare:
Pick a friendly **ORACLES OF FATE WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick 1 of the friendly Manifestations below that is not on the battlefield, then make a casting roll of 2D6.

**• Burning Sigil of Tzeentch**
**• Daemonic Simulacrum**
**• Tome of Eyes**
spells.effect: If there is already a friendly **MANIFESTATION** from the list above on the battlefield, it is immediately banished and removed from play. Then, set up the **MANIFESTATION** you picked within 1" of the caster and visible to them.

Gaunt Summoner on Disc of Tzeentch

armiesOfRenown.name: The Roving Maw
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Where the ground itself groans with hunger and opens in undulating gullets, twisted, feral shapes slaughter errant warriors to feed to the grisly apertures. These are the Gorger Mawpacks, ogors afflicted with a terrible curse that keeps them in a permanent state of starvation. Ogor Butchers will often travel with these creatures in Roving Maws, testing new recipes upon them and opening Mawpits with gastromantic rituals.

When you pick the Ogor Mawtribes faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a The Roving Maw Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Ogor Mawtribes faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options, as described in Army of Renown: The Roving Maw. All units in your army gain the Roving Maw keyword.

rules.name: Driven By Starvation
rules.effect: Friendly **Gorger Mawpack** units can use **CHARGE** abilities even if they used a **RUN** ability in the same turn unless they are wholly within 3" of a terrain feature.

rules.name: Trampling Charge
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **ROVING MAW** unit that charged this turn to use this ability, then pick a visible enemy unit within 1" of it to be the target.
rules.effect: Roll a D3. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.

rules.name: Tasty Morsels
rules.effect: Each time a model is slain by a friendly **Mawpit**’s ‘Throat of Ghur’ ability, you gain 1 **tasty morsel**.

rules.name: Mawpits of Ghur
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle Round (Army), Start of Battle Round
rules.effect:
Roll 2D6 and add the number of **tasty morsels** you have to the roll. Apply the corresponding effect below for the rest of the battle round. Then, reset your **tasty morsels** to 0.

**2-3 – *Subdued*:**
No effect.

**4-5 – *Peckish*:**
Subtract 3 from the control scores of units that are **vulnerable to Mawpits**.

**6-8 – *Rumbling*:**
Roll a dice for each unit that is **vulnerable to Mawpits**. On a 3+, inflict 1 mortal damage on that unit.

**9-11 – *Famished*:**
Units that are **vulnerable to Mawpits** cannot use commands.

**12+ – *Ravenous*:**
Roll a D3 for each unit that is **vulnerable to Mawpits**. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on that unit equal to the roll.

rules.name: The Realm Hungers
rules.effect: Units and **MANIFESTATIONS** are **vulnerable** **to Mawpits** while they are on the battlefield unless they are wholly within 1" of a terrain feature that is not a **Mawpit**. This ability has no effect on **ROVING MAW** units or units that have **FLY**.

artefacts.name: Flasks of Congealed Maw-juices
artefacts.effect: Friendly **Mawpits** within this unit’s combat range have **WARD** **(5+)**.

heroicTraits.name: Prime Gutserver
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle Round (Army), Start of Battle Round
heroicTraits.effect: If this unit is a Head Butcher, gain 1 **tasty morsel**.

spells.name: Mawmeat
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ROVING MAW WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible terrain feature within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: For the rest of the battle, units on or within 1" of the target are **vulnerable to Mawpits**. This ability had no effect on **ROVING MAW** units or units that have **FLY**.

spells.name: Retcher
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ROVING MAW WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the caster’s melee weapons until the start of your next turn.

armiesOfRenown.name: The Swords of Chaos
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Even amongst the Varanguard, the elite champions of the Everchosen, the Swords of Chaos possess a bleak renown. When a foe earns Archaon’s personal displeasure, it is they who are unleashed to render his pitiless judgement. Clad in heraldry of stark black and gold, these anointed knights of Chaos are sworn to their master’s vision of a blasted cosmos over which only his graven banners rise.

When you pick the Slaves to Darkness faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a The Swords of Chaos Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Slaves to Darkness faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options, as described in Army of Renown: The Swords of Chaos. All units in your army gain the Swords of Chaos keyword.

rules.name: Descent From Ruinous Skies
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** unit that is **in the Ruinous Skies**.
rules.effect: Set up that unit anywhere on the battlefield more than 9" from all enemy units.

rules.name: Knights of the First Circle
rules.declare: If there are more friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** units on the battlefield than there are set up in reserve, pick a friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** unit that has not been deployed.
rules.effect: Set up that unit in reserve **in the Ruinous Skies**. It has now been deployed.

rules.name: Demands of the Dark Gods
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Start of Battle Round
rules.effect:
Pick 1 of the following effects:

***Demand of Khorne***: For the rest of the battle round, add 1 to the Rend characteristic of melee weapons used by friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** units.

***Demand of Tzeentch***: Pick 1 visible enemy unit within 9" of a friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** unit that is not in combat and roll a D6. On a 3+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on that enemy unit equal to the roll.

***Demand of Nurgle***: **Heal (D3)** each friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** unit. Then pick a friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** unit that healed any damage points this turn. Until the start of the next battle round, that unit has **WARD (6+)**.

***Demand of Slaanesh***: Add 2" to the Move characteristic of friendly **SWORDS OF CHAOS** units until the start of the next battle round.

other.name: The Grimroot Order
other.effect: Add 1 to this unit’s Health characteristic.

other.name: The Blackstorm Apostates
other.effect: Add 3" to this unit’s Move characteristic.

other.name: Tamers of Haradh's Torment
other.effect: Add 1 to the Damage characteristic of this unit’s **Companion** weapons.

other.name: The Hounds Apocalyptus
other.effect: While this unit is wholly within enemy territory, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of this unit’s melee weapons.

armiesOfRenown.name: Tribes of the Snow Peaks
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Furious are the Darkoath tribes of the Snow Peaks, as their home has been shattered by verminous invaders. United under Chieftain Gunnar Brand and his kin, they now fight enemies on two fronts: the greedy Sigmarites to their backs and the perfidious Skaven of the Gnaw beyond. With every enemy slain, they come closer to fulfilling furious oaths avenging their fallen kin.

When you pick the Slaves to Darkness faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Tribes of the Snow Peaks Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Slaves to Darkness faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options, as described in Army of Renown: Tribes of the Snow Peaks. All units in your army gain the Snow Peaks keyword.

rules.name: Shroud of the Pale Elk
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Phase (Army), Reaction: Opponent declared an ATTACK ability
rules.effect: If that friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit has not used an **OATH** ability this turn, spend 1 **oath point**. For the rest of the turn, that friendly unit has **WARD (4+)**.

rules.name: Hunger of Shesh'shan
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Phase (Army), Your Combat Phase
rules.declare: If you have 1 or more **oath points**, pick a friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit that has not used an **OATH** ability this turn to be the target.
rules.effect: Spend 1 **oath point**. For the rest of the turn, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of the target’s melee weapons.

rules.name: Daemonfire Weapons
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Phase (Army), Your Combat Phase
rules.declare: If you have 1 or more **oath points**, pick a friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit that has not used an **OATH** ability this turn to be the target.
rules.effect: Spend 1 **oath point**. For the rest of the turn, add 1 to the Rend characteristic of the target’s melee weapons.

rules.name: Speed of the Blood Crow
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Phase (Army), Your Charge Phase
rules.declare: If you have 1 or more **oath points**, pick a friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit that has not used an **OATH** ability this turn to be the target.
rules.effect: Spend 1 **oath point**. For the rest of the turn, the target can use **CHARGE** abilities even if it used a **RUN** ability in the same turn.

rules.name: Rage of Arkhar
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Phase (Army), Your Combat Phase
rules.declare: If you have 1 or more **oath points**, pick a friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit that has not used an **OATH** ability this turn to be the target.
rules.effect: Spend 2 **oath points**. The target can use 2 **FIGHT** abilities this phase. After the first is used, however, the target has **STRIKE‑LAST** for the rest of the turn.

rules.name: Messengers of the Gods
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Phase (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: If you have 1 or more **oath points**, pick a friendly **SNOW PEAKS Darkoath Marauders** or **Fellriders** unit that has been destroyed and has not used an **OATH** ability this turn to be the target.
rules.effect: Spend 2 **oath points**. Set up a replacement unit with half the number of models from the target unit (rounding up) on the battlefield, wholly within 6" of a battlefield edge and more than 3" from all enemy units.

rules.name: Pledges to the Dark Gods
rules.effect:
Each time a friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit uses 1 of the following abilities and the effect of that ability is resolved, gain 1 **oath point**.

• ‘Oath of Bloodshed’
• ‘Oath of Murder’
• ‘Oath of Supremacy’
• ‘Oath of the Marauder’
• ‘Oath of the Raider’
• ‘Oath of Conquest’

**Designer’s Note**: *The abilities listed above are abilities on* **DARKOATH** *warscrolls. By using those abilities (i.e. completing the oaths), the player gains* **oath points**, *which can then be spent to use the* **OATH** *abilities in these Army of Renown rules.*

artefacts.name: Grand Offering
artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), Any Movement Phase
artefacts.declare: Pick a friendly **SNOW PEAKS Wilderfiend** within 9" of this unit to be the target.
artefacts.effect: Give the target D6 **sacrifice points**.

heroicTraits.name: Oath of Kinship
heroicTraits.effect: While this unit is within the combat range of another friendly **SNOW PEAKS** unit, add 1 to hit rolls for this unit’s attacks.

armiesOfRenown.name: Trugg's Troggherd
armiesOfRenown.lore:
When Trugg stomps into battle, he does so at the head of a lumbering mass of troggoths, from river-dwelling Fellwaters to mighty Dankholds. Drawn from their foetid lairs by the imposing aura of the Troggoth King, these lumpen brutes pulverise anything in their path, never questioning their monarch’s apparently random course.

When you pick the Gloomspite Gitz faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Trugg’s Troggherd Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Gloomspite Gitz faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options, as described in Army of Renown: Trugg's Troggherd. All units in your army gain the Trugg’s Troggherd keyword.

rules.name: Herd Healing
rules.effect: Each time a friendly **TROGGOTH** unit uses a **FIGHT** ability, after that ability has been resolved, **Heal (D3)** that unit.

rules.name: Moon-toughened Hide
rules.effect: In battle rounds 2 and 3, subtract 1 from the Rend characteristic of melee weapons used for attacks that target friendly **TRUGG’S TROGGHERD** units.

rules.name: Broken Slumber
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **TRUGG’S TROGGHERD** unit that is a **living** **landmark**.
rules.effect: Set up that unit anywhere on the battlefield wholly within 3" of a terrain feature and more than 9" from all enemy units.

rules.name: Living Landmark
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **TRUGG’S TROGGHERD** unit that has not been deployed.
rules.effect: Set up that unit in reserve as a **living landmark**. It has now been deployed.

rules.name: Aura of Haywire Magic
rules.effect: The effects of a friendly **Trugg**’s ‘Malfunctioning Leystone’ ability affect friendly **TRUGG’S TROGGHERD** units anywhere on the battlefield instead of only those wholly within 12" of that friendly **Trugg**.

artefacts.name: Crunchy Shinies
artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Any Movement Phase
artefacts.effect: For the rest of the turn, add 3 to run rolls and charge rolls for this unit.

heroicTraits.name: Loonstone Teef
heroicTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle, Any Combat Phase
heroicTraits.effect: This unit can use 2 **FIGHT** abilities this phase. After the first is used, however, this unit has **STRIKE‑LAST** for the rest of the turn.

armiesOfRenown.name: Vanari Paragons
armiesOfRenown.lore:
In the Tyrionic nations of Hysh, decisive action, martial skill and bravery are valued above all. It is common for their Vanari to seek to emulate the Lord Phoenix by undertaking vows or labours inspired by his own quests in the prehistory of the realms. In so doing, they honour the Decree Tyrionic, to which they owe their existence.

When you pick the Lumineth Realm-lords faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Vanari Paragons Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Lumineth Realm-lords faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Vanari Paragons. All units in your army gain the Vanari Paragons keyword.

rules.name: Rewards of Valour
rules.declare: Pick a friendly non-**UNIQUE VANARI HERO** that does not have an artefact of power to be the target.
rules.effect:
Give the target 1 artefact of power from the 'Vanari Paragons' Army of Renown rules.

**Designer's Note:** *This artefact of power is given to the* **HERO** *during the deployment phase, not during army composition.*

rules.name: Labour of Might

rules.declare: You can only use this ability if 3 or more enemy units have been destroyed this battle.
rules.effect: Add 1 to the number of **labours** you have completed. For the rest of the battle, add 1 to wound rolls for combat attacks made by friendly **LUMINETH REALM-LORDS** units.

rules.name: Labour of Control
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: You can only use this ability if there is at least 1 different friendly unit within 6" of each battlefield edge.
rules.effect: Add 1 to the number of **labours** you have completed. For the rest of the battle, add 2" to the Move characteristic of friendly **LUMINETH REALM-LORDS** units.

rules.name: Labour of Precision
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: You can only use this ability if there are no enemy units with an artefact of power on the battlefield.
rules.effect: Add 1 to the number of **labours** you have completed. For the rest of the battle, add 10 to the control scores of friendly **LUMINETH REALM‑LORDS** units.

rules.name: Blessing of Tyrion
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: You can only use this ability if you have completed 3 **labours**.
rules.effect: For the rest of the battle, friendly **LUMINETH REALM-LORDS** units have **WARD (5+)**.

artefacts.name: Swiftfeather Talisman
artefacts.declare: Pick a friendly **LUMINETH REALM-LORDS** unit wholly within 12" of this unit to be the target.
artefacts.effect: Add 2" to the target’s Move characteristic for the rest of the turn.

artefacts.name: Phoenix Blade
artefacts.effect: This unit’s combat attacks score critical hits on unmodified hit rolls of 5+.

artefacts.name: Nullstone Beads
artefacts.effect: This unit can use the ‘Unbind’ ability as if it had **WIZARD (1)**.

heroicTraits.name: Master Duellist
heroicTraits.declare: Pick a visible enemy **HERO** in combat with this unit to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect:
Roll a dice. On a 4+:

armiesOfRenown.name: Wardens of the Chorrileum
armiesOfRenown.lore:
It is the task of the Isharann order known as the Soul Wardens to maintain the sacred chorrileums. These repositories contain the souls of long-dead Idoneth, including those of the legendary Cythai, and are of the utmost importance to Deepkin society. The soul-reefs are places of prayer and sanctuary that protect fallen Idoneth from the depredations of the Chaos Gods. They are also vital to the defence of the enclaves, for if an Idoneth city is threatened, its Isharann priests can call upon the souls of the Cythai to raise Eidolons of Mathlann and other spiritual wardens to protect it.

So revered are the soul-reefs that a recent decree of Volturnos calling for the Soul Wardens to risk the souls of the Cythai was met with great resistance. Nevertheless, under the guidance of Mathaela, Oracle of the Abyss, the power of the chorrileums is now being siphoned off to enact rituals of awakening, giving form and fury to the terrifying Incarnates of the Deep.

When you pick the Idoneth Deepkin faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Wardens of the Chorrileum Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Idoneth Deepkin faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options as described in Army of Renown: Wardens of the Chorrileum. All units in your army gain the Chorrileum keyword.

rules.name: The Ocean Conceals
rules.declare: This ability must be used to deploy the friendly **CHORRILEUM EIDOLON** instead of the ‘Deploy Unit’ ability or the ‘Deploy Regiment’ ability.
rules.effect: Set up the friendly **CHORRILEUM EIDOLON** in reserve **awaiting a summons**. It has now been deployed.

rules.name: Call Forth the Tsunami
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), Your Combat Phase
rules.declare: You can only use this ability if you have 3 or more **chorrileum spirit points**. Pick a friendly unit **awaiting a summons** to be the target.
rules.effect: Set up the target anywhere on the battlefield. Then, inflict D3 mortal damage on each enemy unit in combat with it.

rules.name: Spirits of the Chorrileum
rules.effect:
Each turn, you can gain up to 1 **chorrileum spirit point**. You gain 1 **chorrileum spirit point** the first time 1 of the following occurs in a turn:

• You make an unmodified casting roll of 8 or more for a friendly **ISHARANN WIZARD**.
• An enemy unit is destroyed while it is within 12" of any friendly **ISHARANN** units.
• A friendly unit is destroyed while it is wholly within 12" of any friendly **ISHARANN** units or while it is contesting an objective you control

rules.name: Wrath of Mathlann
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Combat Phase
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **CHORRILEUM EIDOLON** unit that has not used any **RAMPAGE** abilities this turn to use this ability.
rules.effect: That unit can use 2 **FIGHT** abilities this phase. After the first is used, however, that unit has **STRIKE‑LAST** for the rest of the turn. The unit using this ability cannot use any other **RAMPAGE** abilities for the rest of the turn.

artefacts.effect: Set up a replacement unit with half the number of models from the target unit (rounding up) wholly within 12" of this unit and more than 9" from all enemy units.

heroicTraits.name: Serve the Enclave
heroicTraits.effect: While this unit and a friendly **CHORRILEUM NAMARTI** unit are contesting the same objective, this unit has a control score of 10 that cannot be modified.

spells.effect: The target can move up to D6". That move cannot pass through or end within the combat range of an enemy unit.

armiesOfRenown.name: Zainthar Kai
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Even fellow Khainites fear the coming of the Zainthar Kai, the legendary ‘Blood Saints’ of Morathi-Khaine. Each of these Scáthborn is empowered by droplets of Khaine’s divine ichor, and their veins boil with barely constrained might. They rarely deign to fight with aelven Khainites, whom they view as lesser beings.

The Zainthar Kai are sent to annihilate those who have truly angered Morathi-Khaine.

When you pick the Daughters of Khaine faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Zainthar Kai Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Daughters of Khaine faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Zainthar Kai All units in your army gain the **ZAINTHAR KAI** keyword.

rules.name: Dark Majesty
rules.effect: If the unmodified hit roll for an attack made by a non‑**HERO** unit that targets the friendly **Morathi‑Khaine** is 1-4, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends.

rules.name: The Blood Saints
rules.effect: While the friendly **Shadow Queen** is wholly within 6" of a friendly non-**HERO ZAINTHAR KAI** unit, both that **Shadow Queen** and that non-**HERO** unit have **WARD (5+)**.

rules.name: Matriarch of the Zainthar Kai
rules.declare: Pick the friendly **Morathi-Khaine** to use this ability.
rules.effect:
Pick 1 of the following effects:

• Until the start of your next turn, this unit has a Move characteristic of 12" and has **FLY**.
• This unit can use the ‘Iron Heart of Khaine’ ability as if you had performed a blood rite.
• Pick a visible enemy unit within 18" of this unit to be the target. For the rest of the turn, the target’s attacks cannot score critical hits (treat them as regular hits instead).
• Pick a friendly non-**UNIQUE ZAINTHAR KAI HERO** that has been destroyed to be the target. Set up a replacement unit identical to the target within 3" of this unit.

rules.name: Cursed Heritage
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), End of Your Turn
rules.declare: Pick each enemy unit in combat with any non-**UNIQUE ZAINTHAR KAI** units to be the targets.
rules.effect: Inflict D3 mortal damage on each target.

artefacts.name: Amulet of the Bladed Queen
artefacts.effect: This unit has **WARD (5+)**. Add 1 to save rolls for this unit.

heroicTraits.name: Khaine's Inheritor
heroicTraits.declare: Pick a visible enemy unit within 12" of this unit to be the target.
heroicTraits.effect:
Roll a D3. On a 2+:

• Inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.
• Halve the target’s Move characteristic until the start of your next turn.

spells.name: Ethereal Grace
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Add 2" to the target’s Move characteristic for the rest of the turn.

spells.name: On Wings of Shadow
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Remove the target from the battlefield and set it up again on the battlefield more than 9" from all enemy units.

spells.name: Speed of the Scáthborn
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: The target has **STRIKE-FIRST** for the rest of the turn.

spells.name: Manifestations of Murder
spells.declare:
Pick a friendly **ZAINTHAR KAI WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick 1 of the friendly **MANIFESTATIONS** below that is not on the battlefield, then make a casting roll of 2D6.

**• Bloodwrack Viper**
**• Bladewind**
**• Heart of Fury**
spells.effect: If you picked a **Bloodwrack Viper** or **Bladewind**, set it up wholly within 12" of the caster, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units. If you picked a **Heart of Fury**, set it up wholly within 18" of the caster and visible to them.

armiesOfRenown.name: Ziggurat Stampede
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Alongside the ruling sorcerers stand the Drazghar, or Scorched Sect – a religious order who speak their god’s burning hate and protect his mysteries. These priests wield influence over the bellicose Bull Centaurs, ‘sanctioned’ mutants who revel in violence and contempt. When an enemy has drawn the deepest ire of the duardin, it is these sacred warriors, as well as the daemon-constructs that share their god’s tauroid form, that are assembled as holy stampedes to crush those marked by the Bullfather’s wrath. Faced with the coming of the Helsmiths of Hashut, nothing stands. Not shieldwalls and battlelines. Neither crenellation nor fortress. Certainly not hope.

When you pick the Helsmiths of Hashut faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Ziggurat Stampede Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules in Army of Renown: Ziggurat Stampede instead of the Helsmiths of Hashut faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options box as described in Army of Renown: Ziggurat Stampede. All units in your army gain the ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE keyword.

rules.name: Let The Realms Tremble
rules.effect: Add **X** to charge rolls for friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE** units, where **X** is the number of friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE** units that have already charged this phase.

rules.effect: Any number of friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE** units can use the ‘Power Through’ command in the same turn.

rules.name: Run Roughshod
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE** unit in combat to be the target.
rules.effect:
For the rest of the turn:

•  The target can use **SHOOT** and/or **CHARGE** abilities even if it used a **RETREAT** ability in the same turn.
•  No mortal damage is inflicted on the target by **RETREAT** abilities.

artefacts.name: Visage of the Great Bull
artefacts.effect: In your charge phase, add 1 to the number of dice rolled when making charge rolls for this unit, to a maximum of 3.

heroicTraits.name: Raging Animus
heroicTraits.effect: Each time you make an unmodified save roll of 1 for a combat attack that targets this unit, inflict D3 mortal damage on the attacking unit after the **FIGHT** ability has been resolved.

spells.name: Searing Detonation
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick each enemy unit in combat with them to be the targets, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Roll a D3 for each target. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.

spells.name: Flaming Weapons
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Add 1 to the Rend characteristic of the target’s melee weapons until the start of your next turn.

spells.name: Burn to Ash
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZIGGURAT STAMPEDE WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a terrain feature within 18" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect:
If the target has a Health characteristic, inflict 2D3 mortal damage on it. Then, if the target was not destroyed or does not have a Health characteristic:

• The target gains the ‘Obscuring’ terrain ability for the rest of the battle (Terrain, 1.2).
• If the target is **FACTION TERRAIN**, until the start of your next turn, abilities on that terrain feature’s warscroll have no effect.

armiesOfRenown.name: Zoggrok's Ironmongerz
armiesOfRenown.lore:
There’s talk of strongpoints going dark on the edge of orruk-held badlands. Their ruins are found surrounded by crude siege-works, and their streets are filled with bodies mangled in an almost industrial fashion. The Marshals are stumped. Doesn’t fit standard Ironjawz tactics. Grizzot wasn’t surprised, though. He said he knew exactly who was responsible: Zoggrok Anvilsmasha and his “Ironmongerz”. Anvilsmasha is a rare example of an orruk smith – the brutes are usually content to simply punch plates of metal into roughly the right shape – though I’d expect that he and those he bosses about are interested only in crafting increasingly more destructive weapons rather than anything more productive. Some of the men said they’d never heard of orruks crafting anything. Grizzot smirked that it wasn’t fire Zoggrok forged with but the angry breath of their god. Sounds like exaggeration, but you never quite know when it comes to orruks.

Zoggrok has a reputation for being a joyless sort of orruk. If they are responsible for everything Grizzot claims, though, I imagine that you’ll scarcely find a more formidable infantry column than his Ironmongerz. After all, it takes a certain insane recklessness to force a breach or storm a rampart, and Ironjawz have that in spades. If they really are the same fiends who toppled sacred Fort Gardus alongside King Brodd in the Realm of Life, then they’ve already proved their expertise when it comes to sieges. We can only hope they see better sport in the fortresses of the dark ones than our own stranded outposts.

When you pick the Ironjawz faction for your army, you can choose for it to be a Zoggrok’s Ironmongerz Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Ironjawz faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed in the roster options as described in Army of Renown: Zoggrok Ironmongerz. All units in your army gain the Zoggrok’s Ironmongerz keyword.

rules.name: Ironclad Scrums
rules.effect: Friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ** **Ardboyz** units with 3 or more models have **WARD (5+)** while they are wholly within 12" of a different friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ** **Ardboyz** unit with 3 or more models.

rules.name: Shield of Scrap and Muscle
rules.effect:
While a friendly **Zoggrok Anvilsmasha** is within the combat range of a friendly **Ardboyz** unit:

• That **Zoggrok Anvilsmasha** has **WARD (4+)**.
• Each time you make a successful ward roll for that **Zoggrok Anvilsmasha**, allocate 1 damage point to a friendly **Ardboyz** unit within its combat range after the damage sequence has been resolved (ward rolls cannot be made for those damage points).

rules.name: Into Da Breach!
rules.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), End of Any Turn
rules.declare: Pick a friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ** unit that is more than 9" from any enemy units and had any damage points allocated to it this turn.
rules.effect: That unit can move D6". It cannot pass through the combat ranges of enemy units. It cannot end that move in combat.

rules.name: Legendary Vandals
rules.effect: Melee weapons used by friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ** units have **Anti‑FACTION TERRAIN (+1 Rend)** and **Anti‑WAR MACHINE (+1 Rend)**.

artefacts.name: Da Great Wollopa
artefacts.effect:
If any damage points are allocated to an enemy unit by attacks made by this unit, that enemy unit is **krump’d** until the start of your next turn.

While a unit is **krump’d**:

• Subtract 1 from save rolls for that unit.
• Ignore positive modifiers to save rolls for that unit.

heroicTraits.name: Oi! Back To It!
heroicTraits.effect: If a friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ Ardboyz** or **Brutes** unit wholly within 12" of this unit uses the ‘Rally’ command, you can make 3 additional rally rolls of D6.

spells.name: The Green God's Hammer
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ WIZARD** to cast this spell, pick a visible friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ INFANTRY** unit wholly within 12" of them to be the target, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Until the start of your next turn, add 1 to the number of dice rolled when making charge rolls for the target, to a maximum of 3.

spells.name: Get 'Em, Gork!
spells.declare: Pick a friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ PRIEST** to chant this prayer, then make a chanting roll of D6.
spells.effect:
If the chanting roll was 5-9, pick a visible terrain feature wholly within 12" of the chanter. If the chanting roll was 10+, pick a visible terrain feature wholly within 18" of the chanter instead. That terrain feature is **trampled by Gork** for the rest of the battle.

Roll a D3 each time an enemy unit ends a move within 6" of a terrain feature that is **trampled by Gork**. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on that enemy unit equal to the roll.

spells.name: Summon Gork-Roara
spells.declare: If there is not a friendly **Gork-Roara** on the battlefield, pick a friendly **ZOGGROK’S IRONMONGERZ WIZARD** to cast this spell, then make a casting roll of 2D6.
spells.effect: Set up a **Gork-Roara** wholly within 12" of the caster, visible to them and more than 9" from all enemy units.

battleTactics.name: All Roots Entwined
battleTactics.lore: The cycle will only achieve its true potential when it is brought under your rule.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control every objective that can be controlled on the battlefield.

battleTactics.name: Ambuscade
battleTactics.lore: Send forth skirmishers to lie in wait for the enemy.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more objectives or terrain features that you control, in any combination, are each contested by a different friendly unit that is more than 6" from friendly territory and was not set up this turn.

battleTactics.name: Bold Explorers
battleTactics.lore: Only the brave will plunge deep into the ever-seething heart of Neos.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 3 or more objectives or non-**FACTION TERRAIN** terrain features that you control, in any combination, are being contested by any friendly scout units. Those objectives and terrain features must be within enemy territory.

battleTactics.name: Break Their Defences
battleTactics.lore: The enemy cannot hide away for long…
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control the enemy **hideout**.

battleTactics.name: Bring Them In
battleTactics.lore: Dead or alive, the fugitive will pay for their actions.
battleTactics.rulesText:
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 1 of the following is true:
• There are 3 or more friendly units in combat with the enemy **fugitive**.
• There is no enemy **fugitive** on the battlefield and 3 or more friendly units are contesting the same objective that is wholly outside friendly territory.

battleTactics.name: Claim What's Yours
battleTactics.lore: These lands are yours; force the enemy from them.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are more friendly units within enemy territory than enemy units and there is at least 1 friendly **HERO** wholly within enemy territory.

battleTactics.name: Command From the Front
battleTactics.lore: Commanders must work in tandem to fuel the machine of war.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are 2 or more friendly **HEROES** on the battlefield, all friendly **HEROES** are within 9" of a visible enemy unit and no friendly **HEROES** were slain this turn.

battleTactics.name: Contraband Aqua Ghyranis
battleTactics.lore: The Oakenbrow zealously guard Neos’s many springs of Aqua Ghyranis, but their gaze cannot be everywhere…
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if at least 2 enemy units carrying a Ghyranite Treasure have been destroyed this battle.

battleTactics.name: Courageous Adventurers
battleTactics.lore: The depths of the forest hold many secrets. Make sure they are yours.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if a friendly scout unit that was not set up this turn is contesting a non-**FACTION TERRAIN** terrain feature that you control, that is wholly within enemy territory, and that is more than 6" from friendly territory.

battleTactics.name: Cut Off The Head
battleTactics.lore: Fall upon the leaders of the foe without warning.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if an enemy **HERO** has been destroyed this battle.

battleTactics.name: Daring Rescue
battleTactics.lore: The most respected warriors are those who willingly aid their allies.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if an enemy unit that was in combat at the start of the turn was destroyed by a combat attack made by a friendly unit that charged this turn.

battleTactics.name: Daring Resurgence
battleTactics.lore: Life always fights to endure. Draw upon that vitality now.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you are the underdog this battle round, there is at least 1 friendly unit on the battlefield and at least half of the friendly units on the battlefield (rounding up) used a **FIGHT** ability this turn.

battleTactics.name: Defiant Surge
battleTactics.lore: Use the invigoration that floods you to overwhelm the enemy.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control more objectives than your opponent.

battleTactics.name: Envelop and Strangle
battleTactics.lore: Ensure no corner of the Neosian undergrowth is beyond your reach.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if at least 3 different friendly units are each wholly within 9" of a different corner of the battlefield and only 1 of those corners is wholly within friendly territory. No more than 1 of those units can have been set up this turn.

battleTactics.name: Execute the Plan
battleTactics.lore: Bring your plan to fruition and watch as the enemy army goes down in flames.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control each objective within enemy territory.

battleTactics.name: Feign Weakness
battleTactics.lore: Allowing the foe to have small victories keeps them distracted from the task at hand.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if more friendly units were destroyed this turn than enemy units.

battleTactics.name: Fey Strikes
battleTactics.lore: Slip in and out of the melee like the cackling spites of the deep woods.
battleTactics.rulesText:
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if all the following are true:
• At least 2 friendly units moved as part of a **RETREAT** ability this turn. Those units are **lure** units.
• At least 2 other friendly units charged this turn and at least 1 of those units ended the charge move in combat with an enemy unit from which any lure **units** retreated.

battleTactics.name: Form a Wall
battleTactics.lore: A good defence begins with solid foundations.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more friendly units are wholly outside friendly territory, wholly within 6" of friendly territory and within 3" of each other.

battleTactics.name: Hero of Legend
battleTactics.lore: To complete a grand quest is to see one’s name go down in the annals of history.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control an objective wholly outside your territory that you did not control at the start of the turn and a friendly **HERO** is contesting that objective.

battleTactics.name: Invasive Species
battleTactics.lore: Reach your vines and tendrils deep into the territory of the opposition.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control every objective that can be controlled within enemy territory. If there are no objectives within enemy territory, you complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control every objective that was controlled by your opponent at the start of your turn.

battleTactics.name: Keep the Enemy Close
battleTactics.lore: Keep your motives concealed and the enemy unaware of their role in your plans.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control an objective that is contested by at least 1 enemy unit.

battleTactics.name: Ley Line Taproot
battleTactics.lore: Pulsing with the power of the ley lines, this root glows with the power to work wonders – or horrors.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if at least 3 enemy units carrying a Ghyranite Treasure have been destroyed this battle.

battleTactics.name: Master of Arms
battleTactics.lore: A successful invasion requires skill in all aspects of war.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if the same enemy unit had at least 1 damage point allocated to it in 3 different phases this turn.

battleTactics.name: Master of Strategy
battleTactics.lore: Victory blooms from victory, growing greater by the moment.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there is a different friendly unit wholly within each large quarter of the battlefield, you control more objectives than your opponent, and there are no enemy units contesting any objectives that you control.

battleTactics.name: No Survivors
battleTactics.lore: Ensure that the only tales told of this battle are tales of your victory.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more enemy units were destroyed this turn and you control the enemy **hideout**.

battleTactics.name: Outpost Resupply
battleTactics.lore: A general must be precise as to where and when they assign reinforcements.
battleTactics.rulesText:
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control an objective within enemy territory and that objective is contested by:
• At least 1 friendly unit that did not use a **MOVE** ability this turn
• At least 1 other friendly unit that charged this turn.

battleTactics.name: Purification Rites
battleTactics.lore: New birth sometimes demands a matching price in death…
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are no enemy units within friendly territory and no enemy units within neutral territory.

battleTactics.name: Quick Pursuit
battleTactics.lore: Having been spotted, time is running out for the fugitive.
battleTactics.rulesText:
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 1 of the following is true:
•There are 3 or more friendly units that are not in combat and are within 12" of the enemy **fugitive**.
•There is no enemy **fugitive** on the battlefield.

battleTactics.name: Raiding Party
battleTactics.lore: Ensure the foe knows you will brave their domains.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are 3 or more friendly scout units wholly outside friendly territory.

battleTactics.name: Repel the Attackers
battleTactics.lore: Unleash the fury within and scour the enemy from the field.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are 3 or more friendly units within 3" of the centre of the battlefield that are not in combat.

battleTactics.name: Sacred Centrality
battleTactics.lore: Assemble at the heart of the field, as the druids once did around their holy henges.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are at least 2 friendly units within 3" of the centre of the battlefield that are not in combat.

battleTactics.name: Search Every Inch
battleTactics.lore: Whether lava field, ash-plain or humid jungle, leave no corner unexplored.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there is a different friendly unit wholly within each large quarter of the battlefield, those friendly units are more than 9" from the centre of the battlefield, and no more than 1 of those friendly units was set up this turn.

battleTactics.name: Seize The Paths
battleTactics.lore: Open tunnels through the foliage are a vital boon. Force the enemy from them.
battleTactics.rulesText:
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if there are more friendly units in neutral territory than enemy units.

If there is no neutral territory in the battleplan you are playing, you complete this tactic at the end of your turn if there are no enemy units within friendly territory.

battleTactics.name: Stolen Seedpod
battleTactics.lore: Plucked from the branches of one of the towering trees of Neos, this seed contains a core of raw potential.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if at least 1 enemy unit carrying a Ghyranite Treasure has been destroyed this battle.

battleTactics.name: Surround the Enemy
battleTactics.lore: Encircle the foe as the flames of a conflagration encircle the doomed.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more friendly units that were not set up this turn are each within 9" of a different corner of the battlefield and wholly outside friendly territory.

battleTactics.name: Water With Blood
battleTactics.lore: Sometimes, red must help the green to flourish.
battleTactics.rulesText: You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control an objective that was controlled by your opponent at the start of your turn.
